9	"""RefDistributor: the centralized dispatcher for DP online consumers.
10	
11	One distributor per run (it lives on trainer DP rank 0). It is the ONLY reader
12	of the producer's ref channel and the only holder of consume book-keeping:
13	
14	    source channel -> commit (ONE ledger, dedup) -> optimizer-step windows -> per-rank inbox
15	
16	Each rank reads a private inbox (a plain consume-once :class:`StreamingRefQueue`)
17	and holds no channel offset, no partition math, no ledger — the design goal is a
18	single book-keeping authority, not N. Refs are released one complete DP
19	micro-batch round at a time, but an optimizer window is OPENED only once the
20	whole window is already committed locally: a released round obligates every
21	rank to a full accumulation window, and durable acknowledgements only advance
22	in whole windows, so a window that could not complete would strand refs that no
23	resume can ever acknowledge. An end-of-stream therefore always lands on a
24	window boundary; the sub-window leftover (fewer refs than one global window)
25	is settled without dispatch — drop_last-style, matching the floor semantics of
26	``resolve_online_total_steps`` — and the stream closes cleanly. Note the
27	cold-start consequence: ranks receive their first micro-batch only after the
28	whole first window is captured, so rank-side idle timeouts must cover
29	full-window capture latency, not a single round.
30	
31	The consumed counter on the source channel (the producer's backpressure signal)
32	mirrors optimizer-durable work: each rank acknowledges its OWN inbox only after
33	the gathered SQLite ack/marker succeeds, and the distributor forwards the sum
34	of the inbox sidecars to the source counter. The consumer publishes the global
35	dispatch quantum before capture begins, and the producer rejects a watermark
36	smaller than that first window. Dispatch itself does not count as consumption.
37	
38	If the distributor dies it drops a ``.failed`` sentinel (with the traceback)
39	into every inbox; :class:`InboxChannel` readers raise on it at the next poll —
40	a loud, immediate all-rank failure instead of a silent hang, and distinct from
41	the clean ``.closed`` end-of-stream.
42	"""
43	
44	from __future__ import annotations
45	
46	import logging
47	import os
48	import threading
49	import time
50	import traceback
51	from collections import Counter
52	from typing import Iterable, List, Optional
53	
54	from specforge.runtime.contracts import SampleRef
55	from specforge.runtime.data_plane.streaming_ref_channel import StreamingRefChannel
56	
57	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
58	
59	_FAILED_SUFFIX = ".failed"
60	_INBOX_SUFFIXES = (
61	    "",
62	    ".closed",
63	    ".consumed_count",
64	    ".consumed_count.tmp",
65	    _FAILED_SUFFIX,
66	)
67	
68	
69	class InboxChannel(StreamingRefChannel):
70	    """Reader view of a distributor inbox: fails loudly if the distributor died.
71	
72	    ``StreamingRefQueue.get`` consults ``is_closed()`` every poll cycle, so a
73	    ``.failed`` sentinel converts "distributor thread died" from an unbounded
74	    hang into an immediate RuntimeError on every rank.
75	    """
76	
77	    def is_closed(self) -> bool:
78	        failed = self.path + _FAILED_SUFFIX
79	        if os.path.exists(failed):
80	            with open(failed) as f:
81	                raise RuntimeError(f"ref-distributor died:\n{f.read()}")
82	        return super().is_closed()
83	
84	    def failure(self) -> Optional[str]:
85	        failure = super().failure()
86	        if failure is None:
87	            return None
88	        return f"ref-distributor died:\n{failure}"
89	
90	
91	class RefDistributor:
92	    """Single-authority push dispatcher: source channel -> per-rank inboxes."""
93	
94	    def __init__(
95	        self,
96	        source: StreamingRefChannel,
97	        controller,  # DataFlowController (rank 0's, with the run's durable store)
98	        inbox_dir: str,
99	        dp_size: int,
100	        *,
101	        feature_store,
102	        refs_per_rank_step: int,
103	        refs_per_rank_batch: Optional[int] = None,
104	        skip_ids: Optional[Iterable[str]] = None,
105	        requeued_ids: Optional[Iterable[str]] = None,
106	        worker_id: str = "ref-distributor",
107	        poll_s: float = 0.05,
108	        idle_timeout_s: Optional[float] = None,
109	        clock=time.monotonic,
110	        sleep=time.sleep,
111	    ) -> None:
112	        if dp_size < 1:
113	            raise ValueError(f"dp_size must be >= 1, got {dp_size}")
114	        if refs_per_rank_step < 1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"refs_per_rank_step must be >= 1, got {refs_per_rank_step}"
117	            )
118	        if refs_per_rank_batch is None:
119	            refs_per_rank_batch = refs_per_rank_step
120	        if refs_per_rank_batch < 1:
121	            raise ValueError(
122	                f"refs_per_rank_batch must be >= 1, got {refs_per_rank_batch}"
123	            )
124	        if refs_per_rank_step % refs_per_rank_batch:
125	            raise ValueError(
126	                "refs_per_rank_step must be divisible by refs_per_rank_batch: "
127	                f"{refs_per_rank_step} % {refs_per_rank_batch} != 0"
128	            )
129	        self.source = source
130	        self.controller = controller
131	        self.dp_size = dp_size
132	        self.feature_store = feature_store
133	        self.refs_per_rank_step = refs_per_rank_step
134	        self.refs_per_rank_batch = refs_per_rank_batch
135	        self.dispatch_quantum = dp_size * refs_per_rank_step
136	        self.dispatch_round_quantum = dp_size * refs_per_rank_batch
137	        self.worker_id = worker_id
138	        self.poll_s = poll_s
139	        self.idle_timeout_s = idle_timeout_s
140	        self._clock = clock
141	        self._sleep = sleep
142	        self._skip = set(skip_ids or ())
143	        self._requeued = set(requeued_ids or ())
144	        # Inboxes are EPHEMERAL (the ledger is the durable state): recreate them
145	        # fresh so a restarted run cannot replay a previous attempt's dispatch.
146	        # Rank 0 broadcasts setup success before any rank opens a reader.
147	        os.makedirs(inbox_dir, exist_ok=True)
148	        for rank in range(dp_size):
149	            base = self.inbox_path(inbox_dir, rank)
150	            for suffix in _INBOX_SUFFIXES:
151	                try:
152	                    os.remove(base + suffix)
153	                except FileNotFoundError:
154	                    pass
155	        self._inboxes = [
156	            StreamingRefChannel(self.inbox_path(inbox_dir, rank))
157	            for rank in range(dp_size)
158	        ]
159	        # Continue the producer-visible counter instead of rewinding it after a
160	        # consumer restart. A crash can occur after SQLite commit/feature abort
161	        # but before the rank-local inbox ack; repair that narrow window from
162	        # the durable released prefix before the producer applies backpressure.
163	        consumed = self.source.seed_consumed()
164	        if len(self._skip) > consumed:
165	            self.source.mark_consumed(len(self._skip) - consumed)
166	        self._inbox_consumed = 0  # last forwarded sum of the inbox sidecars
167	        self._window: List[SampleRef] = []
168	        self._window_dispatched = 0
169	        self._stop = threading.Event()
170	        self._thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None
171	        self.finished = False
172	        self.error: Optional[BaseException] = None
173	        self.stats = {
174	            "dispatched": 0,
175	            "skipped": 0,
176	            "duplicates": 0,
177	            "dropped": 0,
178	        }
179	
180	    @staticmethod
181	    def inbox_path(inbox_dir: str, dp_rank: int) -> str:
182	        return os.path.join(inbox_dir, f"inbox-rank{dp_rank}.jsonl")
183	
184	    def _forward_consumed(self) -> bool:
185	        """Mirror optimizer-durable inbox acks onto source backpressure."""
186	        consumed = sum(inbox.consumed_remote() for inbox in self._inboxes)
187	        delta = consumed - self._inbox_consumed
188	        if delta <= 0:
189	            return False
190	        self._inbox_consumed = consumed
191	        self.source.mark_consumed(delta)
192	        return True
193	
194	    def pump(self) -> bool:
195	        """One non-blocking cycle: ingest + dispatch + counter. True on progress.
196	
197	        Sets :attr:`finished` (and closes the inboxes) once the source is
198	        closed-and-drained and no full window remains.
199	        """
200	        if self.finished:
201	            return False
202	        source_failure = self.source.failure()
203	        if source_failure is not None:
204	            raise RuntimeError(f"source producer failed: {source_failure}")
205	        progress = False
206	
207	        raw = self.source.poll()
208	        source_drained = False
209	        if not raw and self.source.is_closed():
210	            # The producer can append its final ref and close after the first
211	            # empty poll. Once close is observed no later publication is valid,
212	            # so this post-close poll is the authoritative drained check.
213	            raw = self.source.poll()
214	            source_drained = not raw
215	        if raw:
216	            progress = True
217	            candidates = []
218	            for ref in raw:
219	                if ref.sample_id in self._skip:
220	                    # Released samples stay skip-listed for the whole replay:
221	                    # the prior attempt settled EVERY publication it polled
222	                    # (ack for the fresh one, immediate settle for duplicates)
223	                    # into the persisted consumed sidecar this attempt seeded
224	                    # from. Settling a re-polled duplicate again would advance
225	                    # consumed past the producer's published accounting and
226	                    # crash its reconciliation; a duplicate the prior attempt
227	                    # never polled merely under-counts, which only tightens
228	                    # backpressure and is safe.
229	                    self.stats["skipped"] += 1
230	                    continue
231	                candidates.append(ref)
232	
233	            fresh = self.controller.commit_samples(self.worker_id, candidates)
234	            fresh_ids = Counter(ref.sample_id for ref in fresh)
235	            for ref in candidates:
236	                if fresh_ids[ref.sample_id]:
237	                    fresh_ids[ref.sample_id] -= 1
238	                    continue
239	                # Duplicate publication within an attempt is idempotent.
240	                self.stats["duplicates"] += 1
241	                if ref.sample_id in self._requeued:
242	                    # Reconciliation already put this unacked sample into the
243	                    # transient queue. Its inbox ack will settle the original
244	                    # publication exactly once.
245	                    self._requeued.discard(ref.sample_id)
246	                else:
247	                    # A same-attempt duplicate never reaches an inbox, so
248	                    # settle that extra publication immediately.
249	                    self.source.mark_consumed(1)
250	
251	        # Release complete DP micro-batch rounds while tracking the surrounding
252	        # optimizer window. Round-robin assignment keeps every rank in lockstep;
253	        # durable acknowledgement still happens only after the full window.
254	        queue = self.controller.sample_queue
255	        while True:
256	            if self._window_dispatched == 0:
257	                # Open a new optimizer window only when the WHOLE window is
258	                # already committed locally. Dispatching the first round
259	                # obligates every rank to a full accumulation window, and acks
260	                # advance only in whole windows — so a window opened without
261	                # its full quantum secured could end mid-accumulation at
262	                # end-of-stream, stranding refs no resume can ever settle.
263	                # Once secured, the window completes within this same loop.
264	                if len(self._window) + queue.depth() < self.dispatch_quantum:
265	                    break
266	            need = self.dispatch_round_quantum - len(self._window)
267	            if need:
268	                self._window.extend(queue.get(need, timeout_s=0.0))
269	            if len(self._window) < self.dispatch_round_quantum:
270	                break
271	            rank_batches: List[List[SampleRef]] = [[] for _ in range(self.dp_size)]
272	            for index, ref in enumerate(self._window):
273	                rank_batches[index % self.dp_size].append(ref)
274	            for inbox, refs in zip(self._inboxes, rank_batches):
275	                inbox.publish_batch(refs)
276	            self.stats["dispatched"] += self.dispatch_round_quantum
277	            self._window_dispatched += self.dispatch_round_quantum
278	            self._window = []
279	            if self._window_dispatched == self.dispatch_quantum:
280	                self._window_dispatched = 0
281	            progress = True
282	
283	        if self._forward_consumed():
284	            progress = True
285	
286	        if source_drained:
287	            self._finish()
288	        return progress
289	
290	    def _finish(self) -> None:
291	        # The dispatch loop only opens an optimizer window once its whole
292	        # quantum is committed locally and then completes it within the same
293	        # loop, so end-of-stream always lands on a window boundary: every
294	        # dispatched ref belongs to a completed window whose acks the ranks
295	        # can durably deliver. Guard that invariant loudly — settling refs of
296	        # a dispatched window here would corrupt the ack accounting.
297	        if self._window_dispatched:
298	            raise AssertionError(
299	                "internal invariant violated: a dispatched optimizer window "
300	                "must complete within the pump cycle that opened it "
301	                f"(dispatched={self._window_dispatched}/{self.dispatch_quantum})"
302	            )
303	        leftover = list(self._window)
304	        self._window = []
305	        queue = self.controller.sample_queue
306	        while True:
307	            batch = queue.get(self.dispatch_quantum, timeout_s=0.0)
308	            if not batch:
309	                break
310	            leftover.extend(batch)
311	        if leftover:
312	            self.stats["dropped"] = len(leftover)
313	            reason = (
314	                f"end-of-stream leaves {len(leftover)} refs, fewer than one "
315	                f"optimizer window (required global quantum="
316	                f"{self.dispatch_quantum}: dp_size={self.dp_size} * "
317	                f"refs_per_rank_step={self.refs_per_rank_step}); settled "
318	                "without dispatch so every rank finishes on a completed window"
319	            )
320	            queue.fail(leftover, reason=reason, retryable=False)
321	            cleanup_errors = []
322	            for ref in leftover:
323	                try:
324	                    adopt = getattr(self.feature_store, "adopt", None)
325	                    if callable(adopt):
326	                        adopt(ref)
327	                    self.feature_store.abort(ref.sample_id, reason=reason)
328	                except Exception as exc:  # best effort before the loud failure
329	                    cleanup_errors.append(f"{ref.sample_id}: {exc}")
330	            self.source.mark_consumed(len(leftover))
331	            if cleanup_errors:
332	                reason += f"; cleanup errors={cleanup_errors}"
333	                raise RuntimeError(reason)
334	            logger.warning("ref-distributor: %s", reason)
335	        for inbox in self._inboxes:
336	            inbox.close()
337	        self.finished = True
338	        logger.info("ref-distributor: finished %s", self.stats)
339	
340	    def run(self) -> None:
341	        """Blocking loop; ends when the source is closed-and-drained or stop().
342	
343	        On error the inboxes are NOT closed (a clean close would read as a
344	        normal end-of-stream and let ranks finish "successfully" on partial
345	        data) — ``_run_guarded`` drops a ``.failed`` sentinel instead, which
346	        every rank's :class:`InboxChannel` raises on at its next poll.
347	        """
348	        last_progress = self._clock()
349	        while not self._stop.is_set() and not self.finished:
350	            progress = self.pump()
351	            now = self._clock()
352	            if progress:
353	                last_progress = now
354	                continue
355	            if (
356	                self.idle_timeout_s is not None
357	                and now - last_progress > self.idle_timeout_s
358	            ):
359	                raise TimeoutError(
360	                    f"ref-distributor: no refs for {self.idle_timeout_s:.0f}s and "
361	                    f"the source channel is still open (producer dead?)"
362	                )
363	            self._sleep(self.poll_s)
364	
365	    def _fail_inboxes(self, exc: BaseException) -> None:
366	        """Poison every inbox so all ranks fail loudly (never a silent hang —
367	        and never a clean close that would masquerade as end-of-stream)."""
368	        text = "".join(traceback.format_exception(type(exc), exc, exc.__traceback__))
369	        for inbox in self._inboxes:
370	            try:
371	                inbox.fail(text)
372	            except OSError:  # best effort; ranks still have idle_timeout_s
373	                logger.exception("ref-distributor: could not poison %s", inbox.path)
374	
375	    def _run_guarded(self) -> None:
376	        try:
377	            self.run()
378	        except BaseException as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - surfaced via self.error
379	            self.error = exc
380	            logger.error("ref-distributor: died; poisoning inboxes: %s", exc)
381	            logger.debug("ref-distributor traceback", exc_info=True)
382	            self._fail_inboxes(exc)
383	
384	    def start(self) -> "RefDistributor":
385	        self._thread = threading.Thread(
386	            target=self._run_guarded, name="ref-distributor", daemon=True
387	        )
388	        self._thread.start()
389	        return self
390	
391	    def stop(self, join_timeout_s: float = 10.0) -> None:
392	        self._stop.set()
393	        if self._thread is not None:
394	            self._thread.join(timeout=join_timeout_s)
395	
396	
397	__all__ = ["InboxChannel", "RefDistributor"]
398

# coding=utf-8
# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.

# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
"""RefDistributor: the centralized dispatcher for DP online consumers.

One distributor per run (it lives on trainer DP rank 0). It is the ONLY reader
of the producer's ref channel and the only holder of consume book-keeping:

    source channel -> commit (ONE ledger, dedup) -> optimizer-step windows -> per-rank inbox

Each rank reads a private inbox (a plain consume-once :class:`StreamingRefQueue`)
and holds no channel offset, no partition math, no ledger — the design goal is a
single book-keeping authority, not N. Refs are released one complete DP
micro-batch round at a time, but an optimizer window is OPENED only once the
whole window is already committed locally: a released round obligates every
rank to a full accumulation window, and durable acknowledgements only advance
in whole windows, so a window that could not complete would strand refs that no
resume can ever acknowledge. An end-of-stream therefore always lands on a
window boundary; the sub-window leftover (fewer refs than one global window)
is settled without dispatch — drop_last-style, matching the floor semantics of
``resolve_online_total_steps`` — and the stream closes cleanly. Note the
cold-start consequence: ranks receive their first micro-batch only after the
whole first window is captured, so rank-side idle timeouts must cover
full-window capture latency, not a single round.

The consumed counter on the source channel (the producer's backpressure signal)
mirrors optimizer-durable work: each rank acknowledges its OWN inbox only after
the gathered SQLite ack/marker succeeds, and the distributor forwards the sum
of the inbox sidecars to the source counter. The consumer publishes the global
dispatch quantum before capture begins, and the producer rejects a watermark
smaller than that first window. Dispatch itself does not count as consumption.

If the distributor dies it drops a ``.failed`` sentinel (with the traceback)
into every inbox; :class:`InboxChannel` readers raise on it at the next poll —
a loud, immediate all-rank failure instead of a silent hang, and distinct from
the clean ``.closed`` end-of-stream.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import os
import threading
import time
import traceback
from collections import Counter
from typing import Iterable, List, Optional

from specforge.runtime.contracts import SampleRef
from specforge.runtime.data_plane.streaming_ref_channel import StreamingRefChannel

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_FAILED_SUFFIX = ".failed"
_INBOX_SUFFIXES = (
    "",
    ".closed",
    ".consumed_count",
    ".consumed_count.tmp",
    _FAILED_SUFFIX,
)


class InboxChannel(StreamingRefChannel):
    """Reader view of a distributor inbox: fails loudly if the distributor died.

    ``StreamingRefQueue.get`` consults ``is_closed()`` every poll cycle, so a
    ``.failed`` sentinel converts "distributor thread died" from an unbounded
    hang into an immediate RuntimeError on every rank.
    """

    def is_closed(self) -> bool:
        failed = self.path + _FAILED_SUFFIX
        if os.path.exists(failed):
            with open(failed) as f:
                raise RuntimeError(f"ref-distributor died:\n{f.read()}")
        return super().is_closed()

    def failure(self) -> Optional[str]:
        failure = super().failure()
        if failure is None:
            return None
        return f"ref-distributor died:\n{failure}"


class RefDistributor:
    """Single-authority push dispatcher: source channel -> per-rank inboxes."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        source: StreamingRefChannel,
        controller,  # DataFlowController (rank 0's, with the run's durable store)
        inbox_dir: str,
        dp_size: int,
        *,
        feature_store,
        refs_per_rank_step: int,
        refs_per_rank_batch: Optional[int] = None,
        skip_ids: Optional[Iterable[str]] = None,
        requeued_ids: Optional[Iterable[str]] = None,
        worker_id: str = "ref-distributor",
        poll_s: float = 0.05,
        idle_timeout_s: Optional[float] = None,
        clock=time.monotonic,
        sleep=time.sleep,
    ) -> None:
        if dp_size < 1:
            raise ValueError(f"dp_size must be >= 1, got {dp_size}")
        if refs_per_rank_step < 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"refs_per_rank_step must be >= 1, got {refs_per_rank_step}"
            )
        if refs_per_rank_batch is None:
            refs_per_rank_batch = refs_per_rank_step
        if refs_per_rank_batch < 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"refs_per_rank_batch must be >= 1, got {refs_per_rank_batch}"
            )
        if refs_per_rank_step % refs_per_rank_batch:
            raise ValueError(
                "refs_per_rank_step must be divisible by refs_per_rank_batch: "
                f"{refs_per_rank_step} % {refs_per_rank_batch} != 0"
            )
        self.source = source
        self.controller = controller
        self.dp_size = dp_size
        self.feature_store = feature_store
        self.refs_per_rank_step = refs_per_rank_step
        self.refs_per_rank_batch = refs_per_rank_batch
        self.dispatch_quantum = dp_size * refs_per_rank_step
        self.dispatch_round_quantum = dp_size * refs_per_rank_batch
        self.worker_id = worker_id
        self.poll_s = poll_s
        self.idle_timeout_s = idle_timeout_s
        self._clock = clock
        self._sleep = sleep
        self._skip = set(skip_ids or ())
        self._requeued = set(requeued_ids or ())
        # Inboxes are EPHEMERAL (the ledger is the durable state): recreate them
        # fresh so a restarted run cannot replay a previous attempt's dispatch.
        # Rank 0 broadcasts setup success before any rank opens a reader.
        os.makedirs(inbox_dir, exist_ok=True)
        for rank in range(dp_size):
            base = self.inbox_path(inbox_dir, rank)
            for suffix in _INBOX_SUFFIXES:
                try:
                    os.remove(base + suffix)
                except FileNotFoundError:
                    pass
        self._inboxes = [
            StreamingRefChannel(self.inbox_path(inbox_dir, rank))
            for rank in range(dp_size)
        ]
        # Continue the producer-visible counter instead of rewinding it after a
        # consumer restart. A crash can occur after SQLite commit/feature abort
        # but before the rank-local inbox ack; repair that narrow window from
        # the durable released prefix before the producer applies backpressure.
        consumed = self.source.seed_consumed()
        if len(self._skip) > consumed:
            self.source.mark_consumed(len(self._skip) - consumed)
        self._inbox_consumed = 0  # last forwarded sum of the inbox sidecars
        self._window: List[SampleRef] = []
        self._window_dispatched = 0
        self._stop = threading.Event()
        self._thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = None
        self.finished = False
        self.error: Optional[BaseException] = None
        self.stats = {
            "dispatched": 0,
            "skipped": 0,
            "duplicates": 0,
            "dropped": 0,
        }

    @staticmethod
    def inbox_path(inbox_dir: str, dp_rank: int) -> str:
        return os.path.join(inbox_dir, f"inbox-rank{dp_rank}.jsonl")

    def _forward_consumed(self) -> bool:
        """Mirror optimizer-durable inbox acks onto source backpressure."""
        consumed = sum(inbox.consumed_remote() for inbox in self._inboxes)
        delta = consumed - self._inbox_consumed
        if delta <= 0:
            return False
        self._inbox_consumed = consumed
        self.source.mark_consumed(delta)
        return True

    def pump(self) -> bool:
        """One non-blocking cycle: ingest + dispatch + counter. True on progress.

        Sets :attr:`finished` (and closes the inboxes) once the source is
        closed-and-drained and no full window remains.
        """
        if self.finished:
            return False
        source_failure = self.source.failure()
        if source_failure is not None:
            raise RuntimeError(f"source producer failed: {source_failure}")
        progress = False

        raw = self.source.poll()
        source_drained = False
        if not raw and self.source.is_closed():
            # The producer can append its final ref and close after the first
            # empty poll. Once close is observed no later publication is valid,
            # so this post-close poll is the authoritative drained check.
            raw = self.source.poll()
            source_drained = not raw
        if raw:
            progress = True
            candidates = []
            for ref in raw:
                if ref.sample_id in self._skip:
                    # Released samples stay skip-listed for the whole replay:
                    # the prior attempt settled EVERY publication it polled
                    # (ack for the fresh one, immediate settle for duplicates)
                    # into the persisted consumed sidecar this attempt seeded
                    # from. Settling a re-polled duplicate again would advance
                    # consumed past the producer's published accounting and
                    # crash its reconciliation; a duplicate the prior attempt
                    # never polled merely under-counts, which only tightens
                    # backpressure and is safe.
                    self.stats["skipped"] += 1
                    continue
                candidates.append(ref)

            fresh = self.controller.commit_samples(self.worker_id, candidates)
            fresh_ids = Counter(ref.sample_id for ref in fresh)
            for ref in candidates:
                if fresh_ids[ref.sample_id]:
                    fresh_ids[ref.sample_id] -= 1
                    continue
                # Duplicate publication within an attempt is idempotent.
                self.stats["duplicates"] += 1
                if ref.sample_id in self._requeued:
                    # Reconciliation already put this unacked sample into the
                    # transient queue. Its inbox ack will settle the original
                    # publication exactly once.
                    self._requeued.discard(ref.sample_id)
                else:
                    # A same-attempt duplicate never reaches an inbox, so
                    # settle that extra publication immediately.
                    self.source.mark_consumed(1)

        # Release complete DP micro-batch rounds while tracking the surrounding
        # optimizer window. Round-robin assignment keeps every rank in lockstep;
        # durable acknowledgement still happens only after the full window.
        queue = self.controller.sample_queue
        while True:
            if self._window_dispatched == 0:
                # Open a new optimizer window only when the WHOLE window is
                # already committed locally. Dispatching the first round
                # obligates every rank to a full accumulation window, and acks
                # advance only in whole windows — so a window opened without
                # its full quantum secured could end mid-accumulation at
                # end-of-stream, stranding refs no resume can ever settle.
                # Once secured, the window completes within this same loop.
                if len(self._window) + queue.depth() < self.dispatch_quantum:
                    break
            need = self.dispatch_round_quantum - len(self._window)
            if need:
                self._window.extend(queue.get(need, timeout_s=0.0))
            if len(self._window) < self.dispatch_round_quantum:
                break
            rank_batches: List[List[SampleRef]] = [[] for _ in range(self.dp_size)]
            for index, ref in enumerate(self._window):
                rank_batches[index % self.dp_size].append(ref)
            for inbox, refs in zip(self._inboxes, rank_batches):
                inbox.publish_batch(refs)
            self.stats["dispatched"] += self.dispatch_round_quantum
            self._window_dispatched += self.dispatch_round_quantum
            self._window = []
            if self._window_dispatched == self.dispatch_quantum:
                self._window_dispatched = 0
            progress = True

        if self._forward_consumed():
            progress = True

        if source_drained:
            self._finish()
        return progress

    def _finish(self) -> None:
        # The dispatch loop only opens an optimizer window once its whole
        # quantum is committed locally and then completes it within the same
        # loop, so end-of-stream always lands on a window boundary: every
        # dispatched ref belongs to a completed window whose acks the ranks
        # can durably deliver. Guard that invariant loudly — settling refs of
        # a dispatched window here would corrupt the ack accounting.
        if self._window_dispatched:
            raise AssertionError(
                "internal invariant violated: a dispatched optimizer window "
                "must complete within the pump cycle that opened it "
                f"(dispatched={self._window_dispatched}/{self.dispatch_quantum})"
            )
        leftover = list(self._window)
        self._window = []
        queue = self.controller.sample_queue
        while True:
            batch = queue.get(self.dispatch_quantum, timeout_s=0.0)
            if not batch:
                break
            leftover.extend(batch)
        if leftover:
            self.stats["dropped"] = len(leftover)
            reason = (
                f"end-of-stream leaves {len(leftover)} refs, fewer than one "
                f"optimizer window (required global quantum="
                f"{self.dispatch_quantum}: dp_size={self.dp_size} * "
                f"refs_per_rank_step={self.refs_per_rank_step}); settled "
                "without dispatch so every rank finishes on a completed window"
            )
            queue.fail(leftover, reason=reason, retryable=False)
            cleanup_errors = []
            for ref in leftover:
                try:
                    adopt = getattr(self.feature_store, "adopt", None)
                    if callable(adopt):
                        adopt(ref)
                    self.feature_store.abort(ref.sample_id, reason=reason)
                except Exception as exc:  # best effort before the loud failure
                    cleanup_errors.append(f"{ref.sample_id}: {exc}")
            self.source.mark_consumed(len(leftover))
            if cleanup_errors:
                reason += f"; cleanup errors={cleanup_errors}"
                raise RuntimeError(reason)
            logger.warning("ref-distributor: %s", reason)
        for inbox in self._inboxes:
            inbox.close()
        self.finished = True
        logger.info("ref-distributor: finished %s", self.stats)

    def run(self) -> None:
        """Blocking loop; ends when the source is closed-and-drained or stop().

        On error the inboxes are NOT closed (a clean close would read as a
        normal end-of-stream and let ranks finish "successfully" on partial
        data) — ``_run_guarded`` drops a ``.failed`` sentinel instead, which
        every rank's :class:`InboxChannel` raises on at its next poll.
        """
        last_progress = self._clock()
        while not self._stop.is_set() and not self.finished:
            progress = self.pump()
            now = self._clock()
            if progress:
                last_progress = now
                continue
            if (
                self.idle_timeout_s is not None
                and now - last_progress > self.idle_timeout_s
            ):
                raise TimeoutError(
                    f"ref-distributor: no refs for {self.idle_timeout_s:.0f}s and "
                    f"the source channel is still open (producer dead?)"
                )
            self._sleep(self.poll_s)

    def _fail_inboxes(self, exc: BaseException) -> None:
        """Poison every inbox so all ranks fail loudly (never a silent hang —
        and never a clean close that would masquerade as end-of-stream)."""
        text = "".join(traceback.format_exception(type(exc), exc, exc.__traceback__))
        for inbox in self._inboxes:
            try:
                inbox.fail(text)
            except OSError:  # best effort; ranks still have idle_timeout_s
                logger.exception("ref-distributor: could not poison %s", inbox.path)

    def _run_guarded(self) -> None:
        try:
            self.run()
        except BaseException as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - surfaced via self.error
            self.error = exc
            logger.error("ref-distributor: died; poisoning inboxes: %s", exc)
            logger.debug("ref-distributor traceback", exc_info=True)
            self._fail_inboxes(exc)

    def start(self) -> "RefDistributor":
        self._thread = threading.Thread(
            target=self._run_guarded, name="ref-distributor", daemon=True
        )
        self._thread.start()
        return self

    def stop(self, join_timeout_s: float = 10.0) -> None:
        self._stop.set()
        if self._thread is not None:
            self._thread.join(timeout=join_timeout_s)


__all__ = ["InboxChannel", "RefDistributor"]

9	"""StreamingRefChannel: a cross-process, append-only ``SampleRef`` stream.
10	
11	The offline disaggregation path hands the consumer a *static* ref manifest
12	(:func:`disagg_ingest.write_ref_manifest`) written once before training. The
13	*online* disaggregated path needs the opposite: the rollout producer commits
14	refs continuously while the trainer consumes them, on a different node. This is
15	that control-plane channel.
16	
17	* **Tensor-free.** Only ``SampleRef`` metadata travels here (asserted on
18	  publish); the feature tensors go through the ``FeatureStore`` (Mooncake), so a
19	  shared *data* mount is never required -- only this small control file.
20	* **Append-only JSONL.** ``publish()`` appends one ref per line and fsyncs;
21	  ``poll()`` tail-reads complete lines from the last offset, buffering a partial
22	  trailing line so a reader never parses a half-written record.
23	* **Consume-once friendly.** The reader marks how many refs it has consumed
24	  (``mark_consumed``) in a sidecar counter the writer reads back, so the producer
25	  can apply backpressure (``in_flight_remote``) without any shared in-process
26	  state -- the same split-process model as the rest of disaggregation.
27	* **Explicit outcome.** ``close()`` drops an EOF sentinel only for a successful
28	  producer. ``fail()`` publishes the producer exception separately, so a remote
29	  trainer cannot mistake a truncated rollout for a normal end of input.
30	* **Peer stop.** The consumer publishes done/failed sentinels. A producer that is
31	  blocked on its in-flight watermark can then stop without process supervision.
32	
33	This is the framework, intentionally filesystem-backed (works over any shared
34	control mount). A networked control plane (Redis/the durable MetadataStore)
35	slots in behind the same publish/poll API later.
36	"""
37	
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import json
41	import os
42	import threading
43	import time
44	from dataclasses import dataclass
45	from typing import Iterator, List, Optional, Sequence
46	
47	from specforge.runtime.contracts import SampleRef, assert_no_tensors
48	from specforge.runtime.data_plane.ref_serialization import ref_from_dict, ref_to_dict
49	
50	_CLOSED_SUFFIX = ".closed"
51	_CONSUMED_SUFFIX = ".consumed_count"
52	_FAILED_SUFFIX = ".failed"
53	_CONSUMER_DONE_SUFFIX = ".consumer_done"
54	_CONSUMER_FAILED_SUFFIX = ".consumer_failed"
55	_CONSUMER_QUANTUM_SUFFIX = ".consumer_quantum"
56	
57	
58	@dataclass
59	class RefPublishTransaction:
60	    """Track the ownership-transferred prefix of one publication batch.
61	
62	    JSONL append is not atomic across a batch. A complete line can also become
63	    visible before its fsync reports failure. The channel increments its
64	    publication counter once the full line has been accepted; the transaction
65	    observes that progress even when ``publish`` raises. Cleanup can therefore
66	    preserve every possibly visible ref and abort only the untouched suffix.
67	    """
68	
69	    channel: "StreamingRefChannel"
70	    refs: tuple[SampleRef, ...]
71	    published_count: int = 0
72	
73	    @property
74	    def published_refs(self) -> tuple[SampleRef, ...]:
75	        return self.refs[: self.published_count]
76	
77	    @property
78	    def unpublished_refs(self) -> tuple[SampleRef, ...]:
79	        return self.refs[self.published_count :]
80	
81	    def commit(self) -> None:
82	        """Publish the remaining suffix, retaining progress if one append fails."""
83	
84	        for ref in self.unpublished_refs:
85	            before = self.channel.published
86	            try:
87	                self.channel.publish(ref)
88	            except BaseException as publish_exc:
89	                transferred = self.channel.published - before
90	                if transferred not in (0, 1):
91	                    raise RuntimeError(
92	                        "reference channel reported invalid publication progress: "
93	                        f"{before} -> {self.channel.published}"
94	                    ) from publish_exc
95	                self.published_count += transferred
96	                raise
97	            else:
98	                self.published_count += 1
99	
100	
101	class StreamingRefChannel:
102	    """An append-only, tensor-free ``SampleRef`` stream over a shared file.
103	
104	    One instance per process. The *producer* calls :meth:`publish`/:meth:`close`
105	    (+ reads :meth:`in_flight_remote` for backpressure); the *consumer* calls
106	    :meth:`poll`/:meth:`stream` (+ :meth:`mark_consumed`). Both point at the same
107	    ``path`` on a shared control mount.
108	    """
109	
110	    def __init__(self, path: str) -> None:
111	        self.path = path
112	        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path)), exist_ok=True)
113	        # producer-side
114	        self._published = 0
115	        # consumer-side
116	        self._read_offset = 0
117	        self._buf = ""
118	        self._consumed = 0
119	        # ``mark_consumed`` is called from both the trainer thread (batch acks)
120	        # and the prefetch worker (failure settlement), so the increment and
121	        # the sidecar publish must be one atomic section.
122	        self._consumed_lock = threading.Lock()
123	
124	    # -- producer ----------------------------------------------------------
125	    def publish(self, ref: SampleRef) -> None:
126	        """Append one tensor-free ref. Durable (fsync) so a remote reader sees it."""
127	        assert_no_tensors([ref])
128	        payload = (json.dumps(ref_to_dict(ref), separators=(",", ":")) + "\n").encode(
129	            "utf-8"
130	        )
131	        fd = os.open(self.path, os.O_APPEND | os.O_CREAT | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
132	        try:
133	            offset = 0
134	            while offset < len(payload):
135	                written = os.write(fd, payload[offset:])
136	                if written <= 0:
137	                    raise OSError("reference channel append made no progress")
138	                offset += written
139	            # Ownership transfers only after the complete newline-terminated
140	            # record has reached the kernel. fsync may still report a durability
141	            # failure after the record is visible, so progress must be observable
142	            # to the surrounding transaction before that call.
143	            self._published += 1
144	            os.fsync(fd)
145	        finally:
146	            os.close(fd)
147	
148	    def publish_batch(self, refs: Sequence[SampleRef]) -> None:
149	        """Append a ref batch with one open/write cycle and durability barrier.
150	
151	        Complete JSONL records count as published as soon as their bytes reach
152	        the kernel, including when a later write or the final fsync fails. This
153	        matches :meth:`publish` ownership semantics while allowing ephemeral DP
154	        inboxes to amortize one fsync across an optimizer dispatch window.
155	        """
156	
157	        refs = tuple(refs)
158	        if not refs:
159	            return
160	        assert_no_tensors(refs)
161	        records = [
162	            (json.dumps(ref_to_dict(ref), separators=(",", ":")) + "\n").encode("utf-8")
163	            for ref in refs
164	        ]
165	        payload = b"".join(records)
166	        record_ends = []
167	        end = 0
168	        for record in records:
169	            end += len(record)
170	            record_ends.append(end)
171	
172	        fd = os.open(self.path, os.O_APPEND | os.O_CREAT | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
173	        try:
174	            offset = 0
175	            published_in_batch = 0
176	            while offset < len(payload):
177	                written = os.write(fd, payload[offset:])
178	                if written <= 0:
179	                    raise OSError("reference channel batch append made no progress")
180	                offset += written
181	                while (
182	                    published_in_batch < len(record_ends)
183	                    and record_ends[published_in_batch] <= offset
184	                ):
185	                    self._published += 1
186	                    published_in_batch += 1
187	            os.fsync(fd)
188	        finally:
189	            os.close(fd)
190	
191	    def begin_publish(self, refs: Sequence[SampleRef]) -> RefPublishTransaction:
192	        """Create an observable batch publication boundary without writing yet."""
193	
194	        return RefPublishTransaction(self, tuple(refs))
195	
196	    def publish_many(self, refs: List[SampleRef]) -> None:
197	        self.begin_publish(refs).commit()
198	
199	    def close(self) -> None:
200	        """Drop the EOF sentinel so the reader's stream() terminates when drained."""
201	        self._write_sidecar(_CLOSED_SUFFIX, "")
202	
203	    def fail(self, reason: str) -> None:
204	        """Publish a terminal producer failure for the remote consumer."""
205	        self._write_sidecar(_FAILED_SUFFIX, reason or "producer failed")
206	
207	    def failure(self) -> Optional[str]:
208	        """Return the producer failure reason, if one has been published."""
209	        return self._read_sidecar(_FAILED_SUFFIX)
210	
211	    def mark_consumer_done(self) -> None:
212	        """Tell a split producer that training completed successfully."""
213	        self._write_sidecar(_CONSUMER_DONE_SUFFIX, "")
214	
215	    def mark_consumer_failed(self, reason: str) -> None:
216	        """Tell a split producer that training failed and it must stop."""
217	        self._write_sidecar(_CONSUMER_FAILED_SUFFIX, reason or "consumer failed")
218	
219	    def consumer_failure(self) -> Optional[str]:
220	        """Return the consumer failure reason, if one has been published."""
221	        return self._read_sidecar(_CONSUMER_FAILED_SUFFIX)
222	
223	    def consumer_stopped(self) -> bool:
224	        """Whether the remote consumer completed or failed."""
225	        return os.path.exists(self.path + _CONSUMER_DONE_SUFFIX) or os.path.exists(
226	            self.path + _CONSUMER_FAILED_SUFFIX
227	        )
228	
229	    def publish_consumer_quantum(
230	        self,
231	        refs_per_optimizer_step: int,
232	        *,
233	        allow_existing: bool = False,
234	    ) -> None:
235	        """Claim and publish the consumer's global optimizer-step ref quantum.
236	
237	        Rank 0 calls this exactly once for a fresh attempt. The producer waits
238	        for the value before capture so its in-flight watermark cannot deadlock
239	        below the number of refs needed for one lockstep optimizer step.
240	        """
241	        quantum = int(refs_per_optimizer_step)
242	        if quantum < 1:
243	            raise ValueError(f"consumer quantum must be >= 1, got {quantum}")
244	        path = self.path + _CONSUMER_QUANTUM_SUFFIX
245	        try:
246	            fd = os.open(path, os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
247	        except FileExistsError as exc:
248	            if allow_existing:
249	                existing = self.consumer_quantum()
250	                if existing == quantum:
251	                    return
252	                raise ValueError(
253	                    f"consumer quantum changed across resume for {self.path!r}: "
254	                    f"existing={existing}, requested={quantum}"
255	                ) from exc
256	            raise ValueError(
257	                f"consumer quantum already exists for {self.path!r}; every online "
258	                "attempt requires a fresh reference channel"
259	            ) from exc
260	        with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as stream:
261	            stream.write(str(quantum))
262	            stream.flush()
263	            os.fsync(stream.fileno())
264	
265	    def consumer_quantum(self) -> Optional[int]:
266	        """Return the consumer's global optimizer-step ref quantum, if ready."""
267	        path = self.path + _CONSUMER_QUANTUM_SUFFIX
268	        try:
269	            with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as stream:
270	                value = int(stream.read())
271	        except FileNotFoundError:
272	            return None
273	        except ValueError as exc:
274	            raise RuntimeError(f"invalid consumer quantum at {path}") from exc
275	        if value < 1:
276	            raise RuntimeError(f"invalid consumer quantum {value} at {path}")
277	        return value
278	
279	    def _write_sidecar(self, suffix: str, value: str) -> None:
280	        tmp = f"{self.path}{suffix}.tmp.{os.getpid()}"
281	        with open(tmp, "w", encoding="utf-8") as stream:
282	            stream.write(value)
283	            stream.flush()
284	            os.fsync(stream.fileno())
285	        os.replace(tmp, self.path + suffix)
286	
287	    def _read_sidecar(self, suffix: str) -> Optional[str]:
288	        try:
289	            with open(self.path + suffix, encoding="utf-8") as stream:
290	                return stream.read().strip() or "unknown remote failure"
291	        except FileNotFoundError:
292	            return None
293	
294	    @property
295	    def published(self) -> int:
296	        return self._published
297	
298	    def consumed_remote(self) -> int:
299	        """How many refs the consumer reports having consumed (cross-process)."""
300	        try:
301	            with open(self.path + _CONSUMED_SUFFIX) as f:
302	                return int(f.read() or "0")
303	        except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError):
304	            return 0
305	
306	    def in_flight_remote(self) -> int:
307	        """published - consumed: the producer's backpressure signal."""
308	        return self._published - self.consumed_remote()
309	
310	    # -- consumer ----------------------------------------------------------
311	    def is_closed(self) -> bool:
312	        """True once the producer has dropped the EOF sentinel."""
313	        return os.path.exists(self.path + _CLOSED_SUFFIX)
314	
315	    def poll(self, max_n: Optional[int] = None) -> List[SampleRef]:
316	        """Return refs appended since the last poll (complete lines only).
317	
318	        Non-blocking: returns whatever is available now (possibly empty). A
319	        partially written trailing line is buffered until its newline arrives, so
320	        a ref is never parsed half-written.
321	        """
322	        try:
323	            with open(self.path, "r") as f:
324	                f.seek(self._read_offset)
325	                chunk = f.read()
326	                self._read_offset = f.tell()
327	        except FileNotFoundError:
328	            chunk = ""
329	        if chunk:
330	            self._buf += chunk
331	        # parse the buffer even when no new bytes arrived -- a previous max_n call
332	        # may have left complete lines buffered.
333	        out: List[SampleRef] = []
334	        while "\n" in self._buf:
335	            if max_n is not None and len(out) >= max_n:
336	                break
337	            line, self._buf = self._buf.split("\n", 1)
338	            line = line.strip()
339	            if line:
340	                out.append(ref_from_dict(json.loads(line)))
341	        return out
342	
343	    def mark_consumed(self, n: int) -> None:
344	        """Record n more consumed refs in the sidecar the producer reads back."""
345	        with self._consumed_lock:
346	            self._consumed += n
347	            # Thread-unique tmp name: a concurrent writer must never observe
348	            # (or os.replace away) another writer's half-written counter file.
349	            tmp = (
350	                f"{self.path}{_CONSUMED_SUFFIX}"
351	                f".tmp.{os.getpid()}.{threading.get_ident()}"
352	            )
353	            with open(tmp, "w") as f:
354	                f.write(str(self._consumed))
355	            os.replace(tmp, self.path + _CONSUMED_SUFFIX)  # atomic counter publish
356	
357	    def seed_consumed(self) -> int:
358	        """Continue an existing consumed counter after a consumer restart."""
359	        with self._consumed_lock:
360	            self._consumed = self.consumed_remote()
361	            return self._consumed
362	
363	    def stream(
364	        self,
365	        *,
366	        poll_s: float = 0.25,
367	        idle_timeout_s: Optional[float] = None,
368	        clock=time.monotonic,
369	        sleep=time.sleep,
370	    ) -> Iterator[SampleRef]:
371	        """Yield refs until the channel is closed AND drained.
372	
373	        Blocks (polling every ``poll_s``) while the producer is still live.
374	        ``idle_timeout_s`` (if set) raises ``TimeoutError`` after that long with
375	        no new ref and no close sentinel -- a liveness guard against a dead
376	        producer.
377	        """
378	        last_progress = clock()
379	        while True:
380	            batch = self.poll()
381	            if batch:
382	                last_progress = clock()
383	                for ref in batch:
384	                    yield ref
385	                continue
386	            failure = self.failure()
387	            if failure is not None:
388	                raise RuntimeError(
389	                    f"StreamingRefChannel {self.path}: producer failed: {failure}"
390	                )
391	            if self.is_closed():
392	                # one final drain after close, then stop
393	                tail = self.poll()
394	                for ref in tail:
395	                    yield ref
396	                if not tail:
397	                    return
398	                continue
399	            if idle_timeout_s is not None and clock() - last_progress > idle_timeout_s:
400	                raise TimeoutError(
401	                    f"StreamingRefChannel {self.path}: no refs for "
402	                    f"{idle_timeout_s:.0f}s and not closed (producer dead?)"
403	                )
404	            sleep(poll_s)
405	
406	
407	class StreamingRefQueue:
408	    """Adapts a :class:`StreamingRefChannel` to the ``SampleRefQueue`` protocol
409	    (``get``/``ack``/``fail``) the ``FeatureDataLoader`` consumes in queue mode.
410	
411	    ``get(n)`` BLOCKS (polling) until ``n`` refs are buffered or the channel is
412	    closed-and-drained, so the trainer streams the whole online run and only sees
413	    an empty batch (-> loop ends) once the producer has closed. ``ack`` advances
414	    the channel's consumed counter (the producer's backpressure signal); the
415	    online disaggregated consumers use a retaining store, so loader release ends
416	    only the read lease. The durable optimizer-boundary controller owns the
417	    later physical feature deletion.
418	
419	    """
420	
421	    # A loader that stops after prefetching must put its never-yielded refs back
422	    # without advancing the durable consumed counter. The retaining disagg
423	    # feature store keeps those refs materializable across that retry.
424	    loader_close_retryable = True
425	
426	    def __init__(
427	        self,
428	        channel: StreamingRefChannel,
429	        *,
430	        poll_s: float = 0.1,
431	        idle_timeout_s: Optional[float] = None,
432	        clock=time.monotonic,
433	        sleep=time.sleep,
434	    ) -> None:
435	        self.channel = channel
436	        self.poll_s = poll_s
437	        self.idle_timeout_s = idle_timeout_s
438	        self._clock = clock
439	        self._sleep = sleep
440	        self._buf: List[SampleRef] = []
441	        # ``get`` may run on the loader-prefetch thread while the optimizer
442	        # boundary acknowledges an earlier prefix on the training thread.
443	        # Track that prefix explicitly so a durable ack can advance channel
444	        # backpressure only for the exact refs it covered.
445	        self._inflight: List[SampleRef] = []
446	        self._inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
447	        self._wait_log_interval_s = float(
448	            os.environ.get("SPECFORGE_STREAM_WAIT_LOG_INTERVAL", 30.0)
449	        )
450	        self._last_wait_log = 0.0
451	
452	    def get(self, n: int, timeout_s: float = 0.0) -> List[SampleRef]:
453	        del timeout_s  # the durable stream blocks until closed, failed, or timed out
454	        return self._get(n)
455	
456	    def get_interruptible(
457	        self,
458	        n: int,
459	        *,
460	        stop_event: threading.Event,
461	    ) -> List[SampleRef]:
462	        """Blocking ``get`` variant that a loader-owned worker can stop.
463	
464	        Normal :meth:`get` retains its closed-and-drained blocking contract.
465	        This variant checks the loader event between channel polls; refs already
466	        buffered locally remain unconsumed and therefore recoverable when the
467	        loader stops.
468	        """
469	        return self._get(n, stop_event=stop_event)
470	
471	    def _get(
472	        self,
473	        n: int,
474	        *,
475	        stop_event: Optional[threading.Event] = None,
476	    ) -> List[SampleRef]:
477	        last_progress = self._clock()
478	        while len(self._buf) < n:
479	            if stop_event is not None and stop_event.is_set():
480	                return []
481	            new = self.channel.poll()
482	            if new:
483	                last_progress = self._clock()
484	                self._buf.extend(new)
485	                continue
486	            failure = self.channel.failure()
487	            if failure is not None:
488	                raise RuntimeError(
489	                    f"StreamingRefChannel {self.channel.path}: producer failed: "
490	                    f"{failure}"
491	                )
492	            if self.channel.is_closed():
493	                drain = self.channel.poll()
494	                self._buf.extend(drain)  # final drain
495	                break
496	            if (
497	                self.idle_timeout_s is not None
498	                and self._clock() - last_progress > self.idle_timeout_s
499	            ):
500	                raise TimeoutError(
501	                    f"StreamingRefQueue {self.channel.path}: idle "
502	                    f"{self.idle_timeout_s:.0f}s with the channel still open"
503	                )
504	            now = self._clock()
505	            if (
506	                self._wait_log_interval_s > 0
507	                and now - self._last_wait_log >= self._wait_log_interval_s
508	            ):
509	                print(
510	                    "[stream-ref-queue] "
511	                    f"{time.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')} waiting "
512	                    f"path={self.channel.path} need={n} buffered={len(self._buf)} "
513	                    f"closed={self.channel.is_closed()} "
514	                    f"since_progress={now - last_progress:.1f}s",
515	                    flush=True,
516	                )
517	                self._last_wait_log = now
518	            if stop_event is None:
519	                self._sleep(self.poll_s)
520	            else:
521	                stop_event.wait(self.poll_s)
522	        if stop_event is not None and stop_event.is_set():
523	            return []
524	        take = min(n, len(self._buf))
525	        out, self._buf = self._buf[:take], self._buf[take:]
526	        with self._inflight_lock:
527	            self._inflight.extend(out)
528	        return out
529	
530	    def ack(self, refs: List[SampleRef]) -> None:
531	        self.ack_ids([ref.sample_id for ref in refs])
532	
533	    def ack_ids(self, sample_ids: List[str]) -> None:
534	        """Acknowledge the exact leased prefix after its durable train ack.
535	
536	        The channel exposes a count rather than per-id acknowledgements, so
537	        accepting an arbitrary subset would make restart accounting ambiguous.
538	        Training consumes refs in lease order; enforce that invariant loudly.
539	        """
540	        if not sample_ids:
541	            return
542	        with self._inflight_lock:
543	            actual = [ref.sample_id for ref in self._inflight[: len(sample_ids)]]
544	            if actual != list(sample_ids):
545	                raise RuntimeError(
546	                    "stream acknowledgement is not the leased prefix: "
547	                    f"expected={actual}, got={list(sample_ids)}"
548	                )
549	            del self._inflight[: len(sample_ids)]
550	        self.channel.mark_consumed(len(sample_ids))
551	
552	    def fail(self, refs: List[SampleRef], reason: str, retryable: bool) -> None:
553	        failed_ids = {ref.sample_id for ref in refs}
554	        with self._inflight_lock:
555	            self._inflight = [
556	                ref for ref in self._inflight if ref.sample_id not in failed_ids
557	            ]
558	        if retryable:
559	            self._buf[:0] = refs  # re-buffer at the front for the next get()
560	        else:
561	            self.channel.mark_consumed(len(refs))
562	
563	    def depth(self) -> int:
564	        return len(self._buf)
565	
566	    def in_flight_ids(self) -> List[str]:
567	        with self._inflight_lock:
568	            return [ref.sample_id for ref in self._inflight]
569	
570	
571	__all__ = ["RefPublishTransaction", "StreamingRefChannel", "StreamingRefQueue"]
572
