1	# coding=utf-8
2	# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
3	#
4	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	#
8	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	"""Disaggregated run assembly used by ``specforge train``.
10	
11	The role is configuration, not a different Python entry point. Producer and
12	consumer processes load the same run file with ``training.role`` set to their
13	respective role. Cross-process transport values intentionally remain environment
14	variables because they are deployment secrets/addresses rather than model
15	hyperparameters.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import json
21	import os
22	import time
23	from typing import Callable, Optional, Sequence
24	
25	from specforge.algorithms.registry import AlgorithmRegistration
26	from specforge.config import Config
27	
28	_PRODUCER_CLAIM_SUFFIX = ".producer_claim"
29	_ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX = ".schedule.json"
30	_ONLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES = (
31	    ".closed",
32	    ".consumed_count",
33	    ".failed",
34	    ".consumer_done",
35	    ".consumer_failed",
36	    ".consumer_quantum",
37	    _ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX,
38	)
39	_OFFLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES = (".done", ".consumed", ".failed", ".consumer_failed")
40	
41	
42	def _write_control(path: str, value: str = "") -> None:
43	    """Atomically publish one small filesystem control record."""
44	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path)), exist_ok=True)
45	    tmp = f"{path}.tmp.{os.getpid()}"
46	    with open(tmp, "w", encoding="utf-8") as stream:
47	        stream.write(value)
48	        stream.flush()
49	        os.fsync(stream.fileno())
50	    os.replace(tmp, path)
51	
52	
53	def _read_control(path: str) -> Optional[str]:
54	    try:
55	        with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as stream:
56	            return stream.read().strip() or "unknown remote failure"
57	    except FileNotFoundError:
58	        return None
59	
60	
61	def _publish_control_failure(path: str, exc: BaseException) -> None:
62	    """Best-effort peer notification that never hides the root exception."""
63	    try:
64	        _write_control(path, f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
65	    except Exception as signal_exc:
66	        print(
67	            f"failed to publish role failure to {path}: {signal_exc}",
68	            flush=True,
69	        )
70	
71	
72	def _claim_fresh_control_path(path: str, suffixes: Sequence[str]) -> None:
73	    """Reject artifact reuse and atomically claim a new producer attempt."""
74	    claim = path + _PRODUCER_CLAIM_SUFFIX
75	    artifacts = [path, claim, *(path + suffix for suffix in suffixes)]
76	    existing = [item for item in artifacts if os.path.exists(item)]
77	    if existing:
78	        raise ValueError(
79	            f"control path {path!r} has artifacts from an existing attempt: "
80	            f"{existing}; choose a new attempt-specific path"
81	        )
82	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path)), exist_ok=True)
83	    try:
84	        fd = os.open(claim, os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
85	    except FileExistsError as exc:
86	        raise ValueError(
87	            f"control path {path!r} is already claimed by another producer; "
88	            "choose a new attempt-specific path"
89	        ) from exc
90	    with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as stream:
91	        stream.write(f"pid={os.getpid()}\n")
92	        stream.flush()
93	        os.fsync(stream.fileno())
94	
95	
96	def _publish_role_assembly_failure(cfg: Config, exc: BaseException) -> None:
97	    """Notify the peer when a role fails before its run closure is returned.
98	
99	    Producer notification is allowed only after this process successfully
100	    claimed the attempt path.  That prevents a typo/retry using an occupied
101	    path from poisoning another live producer's channel.
102	    """
103	    env_name = "DISAGG_MANIFEST" if cfg.mode == "offline" else "DISAGG_REF_CHANNEL"
104	    path = os.environ.get(env_name)
105	    if not path:
106	        return
107	    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
108	        claim = _read_control(path + _PRODUCER_CLAIM_SUFFIX)
109	        if claim != f"pid={os.getpid()}":
110	            return
111	        failure_path = path + ".failed"
112	    else:
113	        failure_path = path + ".consumer_failed"
114	    _publish_control_failure(failure_path, exc)
115	
116	
117	def _primary_rank() -> bool:
118	    return int(os.environ.get("RANK", "0")) == 0
119	
120	
121	def _env(name: str) -> str:
122	    value = os.environ.get(name)
123	    if not value:
124	        raise ValueError(f"disaggregated training requires environment variable {name}")
125	    return value
126	
127	
128	def _mooncake_store(cfg: Config, *, retain_on_release: bool = False):
129	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disaggregated import AuthPolicy
130	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.mooncake_store import MooncakeFeatureStore
131	
132	    token = os.environ.get("DISAGG_AUTH_TOKEN") or None
133	    setup_kwargs = {
134	        "local_hostname": os.environ.get("MOONCAKE_LOCAL_HOSTNAME", "127.0.0.1"),
135	        "metadata_server": _env("MOONCAKE_METADATA_SERVER"),
136	        "master_server_addr": _env("MOONCAKE_MASTER_SERVER_ADDR"),
137	        "protocol": os.environ.get("MOONCAKE_PROTOCOL", "tcp"),
138	        "rdma_devices": os.environ.get("MOONCAKE_RDMA_DEVICES", ""),
139	    }
140	    for env_name, key in (
141	        ("MOONCAKE_GLOBAL_SEGMENT_SIZE", "global_segment_size"),
142	        ("DISAGG_CLIENT_SEGMENT_SIZE", "global_segment_size"),
143	        ("MOONCAKE_LOCAL_BUFFER_SIZE", "local_buffer_size"),
144	        ("DISAGG_CLIENT_BUFFER_SIZE", "local_buffer_size"),
145	    ):
146	        if os.environ.get(env_name):
147	            setup_kwargs[key] = int(os.environ[env_name])
148	    return MooncakeFeatureStore(
149	        store_id=os.environ.get("DISAGG_STORE_ID", cfg.run_id),
150	        setup_kwargs=setup_kwargs,
151	        auth=AuthPolicy(token),
152	        credential=token,
153	        retain_on_release=retain_on_release,
154	    )
155	
156	
157	def _offline_store(cfg: Config, *, retain_on_release: bool = False):
158	    backend = os.environ.get("DISAGG_BACKEND", "shared_dir")
159	    if backend == "mooncake":
160	        return _mooncake_store(cfg, retain_on_release=retain_on_release)
161	    if backend != "shared_dir":
162	        raise ValueError(
163	            f"unknown DISAGG_BACKEND={backend!r}; expected shared_dir or mooncake"
164	        )
165	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disaggregated import (
166	        AuthPolicy,
167	        SharedDirFeatureStore,
168	    )
169	
170	    token = os.environ.get("DISAGG_AUTH_TOKEN") or None
171	    return SharedDirFeatureStore(
172	        _env("DISAGG_STORE_ROOT"),
173	        store_id=os.environ.get("DISAGG_STORE_ID", cfg.run_id),
174	        auth=AuthPolicy(token),
175	        credential=token,
176	        retain_on_release=retain_on_release,
177	    )
178	
179	
180	def _server_urls(cfg: Config) -> list[str]:
181	    deployment = cfg.deployment.disaggregated
182	    if deployment is not None and deployment.server_urls:
183	        return list(deployment.server_urls)
184	    raw = os.environ.get("DISAGG_SERVER_URLS") or os.environ.get("DISAGG_SERVER_URL")
185	    if not raw:
186	        raise ValueError(
187	            "online producer requires deployment.disaggregated.server_urls, "
188	            "DISAGG_SERVER_URLS, or DISAGG_SERVER_URL"
189	        )
190	    return [item.strip() for item in raw.split(",") if item.strip()]
191	
192	
193	def _consumer_database_path(cfg: Config) -> Optional[str]:
194	    configured = os.environ.get("DISAGG_DB")
195	    if configured:
196	        return configured
197	    deployment = cfg.deployment.disaggregated
198	    if deployment is None:
199	        return None
200	    state_dir = deployment.consumer_state_dir or deployment.control_dir
201	    return os.path.join(state_dir, "consumer.sqlite")
202	
203	
204	def _online_schedule_payload(cfg: Config, *, num_prompts: int) -> dict:
205	    """Describe the exact finite online schedule prepared by the producer."""
206	    from specforge.training.schedule import resolve_online_total_steps
207	
208	    trainer = cfg.deployment.trainer
209	    dp_size = trainer.nnodes * trainer.nproc_per_node
210	    total_steps = resolve_online_total_steps(
211	        num_prompts=num_prompts,
212	        prompt_epochs=cfg.training.num_epochs,
213	        dp_size=dp_size,
214	        batch_size=cfg.training.batch_size,
215	        accumulation_steps=cfg.training.accumulation_steps,
216	    )
217	    return {
218	        "version": 1,
219	        "total_steps": total_steps,
220	        "num_prompts": num_prompts,
221	        "prompt_epochs": cfg.training.num_epochs,
222	        "prompt_seed": cfg.training.seed,
223	        "dp_size": dp_size,
224	        "batch_size": cfg.training.batch_size,
225	        "accumulation_steps": cfg.training.accumulation_steps,
226	    }
227	
228	
229	def _read_online_total_steps(cfg: Config, channel_path: str) -> int:
230	    """Read and validate the producer-owned online schedule contract."""
231	    schedule_path = channel_path + _ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX
232	    _wait_for(
233	        schedule_path,
234	        timeout_s=_optional_timeout_s("DISAGG_PEER_WAIT_TIMEOUT"),
235	        failure_path=channel_path + ".failed",
236	    )
237	    try:
238	        with open(schedule_path, encoding="utf-8") as stream:
239	            payload = json.load(stream)
240	    except (OSError, ValueError) as exc:
241	        raise ValueError(
242	            f"invalid online schedule record {schedule_path}: {exc}"
243	        ) from exc
244	
245	    trainer = cfg.deployment.trainer
246	    expected = {
247	        "version": 1,
248	        "prompt_epochs": cfg.training.num_epochs,
249	        "prompt_seed": cfg.training.seed,
250	        "dp_size": trainer.nnodes * trainer.nproc_per_node,
251	        "batch_size": cfg.training.batch_size,
252	        "accumulation_steps": cfg.training.accumulation_steps,
253	    }
254	    mismatches = {
255	        key: (payload.get(key), value)
256	        for key, value in expected.items()
257	        if payload.get(key) != value
258	    }
259	    if mismatches:
260	        raise ValueError(
261	            f"online schedule record {schedule_path} does not match this "
262	            f"consumer configuration: {mismatches}"
263	        )
264	    total_steps = payload.get("total_steps")
265	    if not isinstance(total_steps, int) or total_steps < 1:
266	        raise ValueError(
267	            f"online schedule record {schedule_path} has invalid "
268	            f"total_steps={total_steps!r}"
269	        )
270	    return total_steps
271	
272	
273	def _optional_timeout_s(name: str) -> Optional[float]:
274	    """Read an explicitly configured positive timeout.
275	
276	    Long-running producer/consumer coordination is unbounded by default.  A
277	    deployment may opt into a terminal timeout through the corresponding
278	    environment variable, but zero and negative values are almost certainly a
279	    configuration mistake and match neither the schema nor useful semantics.
280	    """
281	    raw = os.environ.get(name)
282	    if raw is None or not raw.strip():
283	        return None
284	    try:
285	        timeout_s = float(raw)
286	    except ValueError as exc:
287	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a positive number, got {raw!r}") from exc
288	    if timeout_s <= 0:
289	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be positive, got {raw!r}")
290	    return timeout_s
291	
292	
293	def _wait_for(
294	    path: str,
295	    *,
296	    timeout_s: Optional[float] = None,
297	    failure_path: Optional[str] = None,
298	) -> None:
299	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout_s if timeout_s is not None else None
300	    while not os.path.exists(path):
301	        failure = _read_control(failure_path) if failure_path else None
302	        if failure is not None:
303	            raise RuntimeError(
304	                f"remote role failed while waiting for {path}: {failure}"
305	            )
306	        if deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
307	            raise TimeoutError(f"timed out waiting for {path}")
308	        time.sleep(0.25)
309	
310	
311	def _hold_mooncake_producer(manifest: str) -> None:
312	    if os.environ.get("DISAGG_BACKEND", "shared_dir") != "mooncake":
313	        return
314	    consumed = manifest + ".consumed"
315	    _wait_for(
316	        consumed,
317	        timeout_s=_optional_timeout_s("DISAGG_PRODUCER_HOLD_S"),
318	        failure_path=manifest + ".consumer_failed",
319	    )
320	
321	
322	def _cleanup_offline_mooncake_refs(store, refs: Sequence, *, reason: str) -> None:
323	    """Remove every object owned by one terminal offline producer attempt."""
324	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.feature_store import drain_feature_store_removals
325	
326	    cleanup_errors = []
327	    for ref in refs:
328	        try:
329	            store.abort(ref.sample_id, reason=reason)
330	        except Exception as exc:
331	            cleanup_errors.append(f"{ref.sample_id}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
332	    try:
333	        drain_feature_store_removals(store)
334	    except Exception as exc:
335	        cleanup_errors.append(f"pending-remove drain: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
336	    if cleanup_errors:
337	        raise RuntimeError(
338	            "offline Mooncake cleanup did not remove every ingested sample: "
339	            f"{cleanup_errors}"
340	        )
341	
342	
343	def _build_offline(
344	    cfg: Config,
345	    *,
346	    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
347	    build_model_bundle: Callable,
348	    optimizer_factory: Callable,
349	    logger: Callable,
350	):
351	    from specforge.training.assembly import (
352	        TrainingRun,
353	        _dataloader_num_workers,
354	        _profiling_options,
355	    )
356	
357	    manifest = _env("DISAGG_MANIFEST")
358	    done = manifest + ".done"
359	
360	    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
361	        _claim_fresh_control_path(manifest, _OFFLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES)
362	
363	        def produce() -> int:
364	            from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disagg_ingest import (
365	                ingest_offline_features,
366	                write_ref_manifest,
367	            )
368	
369	            store = None
370	            tracked_refs = []
371	            produced = 0
372	            primary_exc = None
373	            try:
374	                store = _offline_store(cfg)
375	                refs = ingest_offline_features(
376	                    store,
377	                    cfg.data.hidden_states_path,
378	                    algorithm_name=algorithm.name,
379	                    build_reader=algorithm.providers.offline_for(
380	                        cfg.model.input_modality
381	                    ).build_reader,
382	                    run_id=cfg.run_id,
383	                    ttt_length=cfg.training.ttt_length,
384	                    max_len=cfg.data.max_length,
385	                    on_ref=tracked_refs.append,
386	                )
387	                produced = len(refs)
388	                write_ref_manifest(refs, manifest)
389	                _write_control(done)
390	                _hold_mooncake_producer(manifest)
391	            except BaseException as exc:
392	                primary_exc = exc
393	                # Publish the failure sentinel BEFORE the Mooncake abort sweep:
394	                # the sweep can take minutes over thousands of refs, and a
395	                # supervisor SIGKILL at grace expiry mid-sweep must not leave a
396	                # remote consumer waiting forever on a sentinel that never
397	                # arrives. A cleanup failure below overwrites this record with
398	                # the combined error.
399	                _publish_control_failure(manifest + ".failed", primary_exc)
400	
401	            cleanup_exc = None
402	            if (
403	                store is not None
404	                and os.environ.get("DISAGG_BACKEND", "shared_dir") == "mooncake"
405	            ):
406	                try:
407	                    _cleanup_offline_mooncake_refs(
408	                        store,
409	                        tracked_refs,
410	                        reason=(
411	                            "offline-attempt-failed"
412	                            if primary_exc is not None
413	                            else "offline-attempt-finished"
414	                        ),
415	                    )
416	                except Exception as exc:
417	                    cleanup_exc = exc
418	
419	            if primary_exc is not None and cleanup_exc is not None:
420	                combined = RuntimeError(
421	                    f"offline producer failed ({type(primary_exc).__name__}: "
422	                    f"{primary_exc}) and Mooncake cleanup also failed "
423	                    f"({type(cleanup_exc).__name__}: {cleanup_exc})"
424	                )
425	                _publish_control_failure(manifest + ".failed", combined)
426	                raise combined from primary_exc
427	            if primary_exc is not None:
428	                raise primary_exc
429	            if cleanup_exc is not None:
430	                _publish_control_failure(manifest + ".failed", cleanup_exc)
431	                raise cleanup_exc
432	            return produced
433	
434	        return TrainingRun(execute=produce)
435	
436	    _wait_for(done, failure_path=manifest + ".failed")
437	    from specforge.launch import build_disagg_offline_runtime
438	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disagg_ingest import read_ref_manifest
439	
440	    bundle = build_model_bundle(cfg)
441	    accumulation_steps = cfg.training.accumulation_steps
442	    if cfg.training.attention_backend == "usp":
443	        accumulation_steps *= cfg.training.sp_ulysses_size * cfg.training.sp_ring_size
444	    trainer = build_disagg_offline_runtime(
445	        algorithm=algorithm,
446	        modality=cfg.model.input_modality,
447	        feature_store=_offline_store(cfg, retain_on_release=True),
448	        refs=read_ref_manifest(manifest),
449	        draft_model=bundle.model,
450	        target_head=bundle.target_head,
451	        optimizer_factory=optimizer_factory(cfg),
452	        run_id=cfg.run_id,
453	        output_dir=cfg.output_dir,
454	        ttt_length=cfg.training.ttt_length,
455	        max_len=cfg.data.max_length,
456	        batch_size=cfg.training.batch_size,
457	        accumulation_steps=accumulation_steps,
458	        num_epochs=cfg.training.num_epochs,
459	        max_steps=cfg.training.max_steps,
460	        total_steps=cfg.training.total_steps,
461	        save_interval=cfg.training.save_interval,
462	        eval_interval=cfg.training.eval_interval,
463	        eval_hidden_states_path=cfg.data.eval_hidden_states_path or None,
464	        logger=logger,
465	        log_interval=cfg.training.log_interval,
466	        strategy_kwargs=bundle.strategy_kwargs,
467	        resume_from=cfg.training.resume_from,
468	        max_checkpoints=cfg.training.max_checkpoints,
469	        tp_size=cfg.training.tp_size,
470	        sp_ulysses_size=cfg.training.sp_ulysses_size,
471	        sp_ring_size=cfg.training.sp_ring_size,
472	        use_usp_preprocess=(cfg.training.attention_backend == "usp"),
473	        seed=cfg.training.seed,
474	        dataloader_num_workers=_dataloader_num_workers(cfg, algorithm),
475	        profiling_options=_profiling_options(cfg),
476	    )
477	
478	    def mark_consumed(_step: int) -> None:
479	        if _primary_rank():
480	            _write_control(manifest + ".consumed")
481	
482	    def mark_consumer_failed(exc: BaseException) -> None:
483	        _publish_control_failure(manifest + ".consumer_failed", exc)
484	
485	    return TrainingRun(
486	        trainer=trainer,
487	        on_success=mark_consumed,
488	        on_failure=mark_consumer_failed,
489	    )
490	
491	
492	def _producer_capture_metadata(cfg: Config, algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration):
493	    from specforge.training.capture_contract import resolve_server_capture_contract
494	
495	    contract = resolve_server_capture_contract(cfg, algorithm=algorithm)
496	    return (
497	        list(contract.aux_layer_ids),
498	        contract.target_hidden_size,
499	        contract.target_vocab_size,
500	        contract.draft_vocab_size,
501	    )
502	
503	
504	def _build_online(
505	    cfg: Config,
506	    *,
507	    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
508	    build_model_bundle: Callable,
509	    prepare_prompts: Callable,
510	    optimizer_factory: Callable,
511	    logger: Callable,
512	):
513	    from specforge.training.assembly import (
514	        TrainingRun,
515	        _dataloader_num_workers,
516	        _load_input_tools,
517	        _profiling_options,
518	    )
519	
520	    modality = cfg.model.input_modality
521	    streaming = algorithm.providers.server_streaming_for(modality)
522	    channel_path = _env("DISAGG_REF_CHANNEL")
523	    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
524	        _claim_fresh_control_path(channel_path, _ONLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES)
525	    # The producer owns capture and explicit attempt cleanup. The consumer must
526	    # retain materialized features until DPAckController commits the optimizer
527	    # boundary and explicitly aborts the acknowledged ids.
528	    store = _mooncake_store(cfg, retain_on_release=cfg.training.role == "consumer")
529	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.feature_store import drain_feature_store_removals
530	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.streaming_ref_channel import StreamingRefChannel
531	
532	    channel = StreamingRefChannel(channel_path)
533	
534	    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
535	        from specforge.inference.adapters.server_capture import (
536	            ServerCaptureSchema,
537	            SGLangServerCaptureAdapter,
538	        )
539	        from specforge.launch import build_disagg_online_producer
540	        from specforge.training.model_loading import resolve_draft_config
541	
542	        input_adapter = streaming.create_input_adapter(cfg)
543	        input_tools = _load_input_tools(
544	            cfg,
545	            algorithm,
546	            input_adapter=input_adapter,
547	        )
548	        draft_config = resolve_draft_config(
549	            cfg,
550	            provider=algorithm.providers.model.draft_config,
551	        )
552	        if input_adapter is None:
553	            prompts = prepare_prompts(
554	                cfg,
555	                input_tools,
556	                draft_config=draft_config,
557	            )
558	        else:
559	            prompts = input_adapter.prepare_prompts(
560	                cfg,
561	                input_tools,
562	                draft_config=draft_config,
563	            )
564	        if cfg.training.total_steps is None and cfg.training.max_steps is None:
565	            schedule = _online_schedule_payload(cfg, num_prompts=len(prompts))
566	            _write_control(
567	                channel_path + _ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX,
568	                json.dumps(schedule, sort_keys=True),
569	            )
570	        layers, hidden_size, target_vocab, draft_vocab = _producer_capture_metadata(
571	            cfg, algorithm
572	        )
573	        layout = streaming.layout
574	        capture_schema = ServerCaptureSchema(
575	            aux_feature=layout.aux_feature,
576	            last_hidden_feature=layout.last_hidden_feature,
577	            passthrough=layout.passthrough,
578	            attention_mask_feature=layout.attention_mask_feature,
579	        )
580	        adapters = [
581	            SGLangServerCaptureAdapter(
582	                url,
583	                store,
584	                run_id=cfg.run_id,
585	                algorithm=algorithm.name,
586	                schema=capture_schema,
587	                request_input_adapter=input_adapter,
588	                target_model_version=cfg.model.target_model_path,
589	            )
590	            for url in _server_urls(cfg)
591	        ]
592	        target_repr = streaming.target_representation
593	        peer_wait_timeout_s = _optional_timeout_s("DISAGG_PEER_WAIT_TIMEOUT")
594	        high_watermark_override = os.environ.get("DISAGG_IN_FLIGHT_HIGH_WATERMARK")
595	        in_flight_high_watermark = int(
596	            high_watermark_override or cfg.runtime.in_flight_high_watermark
597	        )
598	        low_watermark_override = os.environ.get("DISAGG_IN_FLIGHT_LOW_WATERMARK")
599	        # Preserve the legacy one-watermark environment override: when only the
600	        # old high value is supplied, resume at that same threshold.
601	        in_flight_low_watermark = (
602	            int(low_watermark_override)
603	            if low_watermark_override is not None
604	            else (
605	                None
606	                if high_watermark_override is not None
607	                else cfg.runtime.in_flight_low_watermark
608	            )
609	        )
610	        _workers, drive = build_disagg_online_producer(
611	            algorithm=algorithm,
612	            modality=modality,
613	            prompts=prompts,
614	            feature_store=store,
615	            channel=channel,
616	            feature_source=adapters if len(adapters) > 1 else adapters[0],
617	            num_rollout_workers=len(adapters),
618	            run_id=cfg.run_id,
619	            target_hidden_size=hidden_size,
620	            target_vocab_size=target_vocab,
621	            draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab,
622	            target_repr=target_repr,
623	            aux_hidden_state_layer_ids=layers,
624	            prompt_epochs=cfg.training.num_epochs,
625	            prompt_seed=cfg.training.seed,
626	            lease=cfg.runtime.producer_lease,
627	            in_flight_high_watermark=in_flight_high_watermark,
628	            in_flight_low_watermark=in_flight_low_watermark,
629	            resident_high_watermark_bytes=(
630	                int(os.environ["DISAGG_RESIDENT_HIGH_WATERMARK_BYTES"])
631	                if os.environ.get("DISAGG_RESIDENT_HIGH_WATERMARK_BYTES")
632	                else cfg.runtime.resident_high_watermark_bytes
633	            ),
634	            resident_low_watermark_bytes=(
635	                int(os.environ["DISAGG_RESIDENT_LOW_WATERMARK_BYTES"])
636	                if os.environ.get("DISAGG_RESIDENT_LOW_WATERMARK_BYTES")
637	                else cfg.runtime.resident_low_watermark_bytes
638	            ),
639	            feature_store_max_resident_bytes=(
640	                cfg.runtime.feature_store_max_resident_bytes
641	            ),
642	            peer_wait_timeout_s=peer_wait_timeout_s,
643	        )
644	
645	        def produce() -> int:
646	            produced = 0
647	            primary_exc = None
648	            try:
649	                produced = drive(should_stop=channel.consumer_stopped)
650	                deadline = (
651	                    time.monotonic() + peer_wait_timeout_s
652	                    if peer_wait_timeout_s is not None
653	                    else None
654	                )
655	                while not channel.consumer_stopped():
656	                    if deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
657	                        raise TimeoutError(
658	                            "producer timed out waiting for the consumer result "
659	                            f"after {peer_wait_timeout_s:.0f}s"
660	                        )
661	                    time.sleep(0.25)
662	                consumer_failure = channel.consumer_failure()
663	                if consumer_failure is not None:
664	                    raise RuntimeError(f"consumer failed: {consumer_failure}")
665	            except BaseException as exc:
666	                primary_exc = exc
667	                try:
668	                    channel.fail(f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
669	                except Exception as signal_exc:
670	                    print(
671	                        f"failed to publish producer failure: {signal_exc}",
672	                        flush=True,
673	                    )
674	            cleanup_errors = []
675	            try:
676	                reader = StreamingRefChannel(channel_path)
677	                while True:
678	                    refs = reader.poll(max_n=1024)
679	                    if not refs:
680	                        break
681	                    for ref in refs:
682	                        try:
683	                            store.abort(ref.sample_id, reason="online-attempt-finished")
684	                        except Exception as exc:
685	                            cleanup_errors.append(
686	                                f"{ref.sample_id}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
687	                            )
688	            except Exception as exc:
689	                cleanup_errors.append(
690	                    f"published-ref scan: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
691	                )
692	            try:
693	                store.discard_external_attempts(
694	                    reason="online-attempt-unadopted-capture"
695	                )
696	            except Exception as exc:
697	                cleanup_errors.append(
698	                    f"unadopted-capture cleanup: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
699	                )
700	            try:
701	                drain_feature_store_removals(store)
702	            except Exception as exc:
703	                cleanup_errors.append(
704	                    f"pending-remove drain: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
705	                )
706	            if primary_exc is not None and cleanup_errors:
707	                raise RuntimeError(
708	                    f"producer failed ({type(primary_exc).__name__}: "
709	                    f"{primary_exc}) and Mooncake cleanup also failed: "
710	                    f"{cleanup_errors}"
711	                ) from primary_exc
712	            if primary_exc is not None:
713	                raise primary_exc
714	            if cleanup_errors:
715	                raise RuntimeError(
716	                    "producer could not clean all published Mooncake features: "
717	                    f"{cleanup_errors}"
718	                )
719	            return produced
720	
721	        return TrainingRun(execute=produce)
722	
723	    from specforge.launch import build_disagg_online_consumer
724	
725	    total_steps = cfg.training.total_steps
726	    if total_steps is None and cfg.training.max_steps is None:
727	        total_steps = _read_online_total_steps(cfg, channel_path)
728	    bundle = build_model_bundle(cfg)
729	    trainer = build_disagg_online_consumer(
730	        algorithm=algorithm,
731	        modality=modality,
732	        feature_store=store,
733	        channel=channel,
734	        draft_model=bundle.model,
735	        target_head=bundle.target_head,
736	        optimizer_factory=optimizer_factory(cfg),
737	        run_id=cfg.run_id,
738	        output_dir=cfg.output_dir,
739	        batch_size=cfg.training.batch_size,
740	        accumulation_steps=cfg.training.accumulation_steps,
741	        max_steps=cfg.training.max_steps,
742	        total_steps=total_steps,
743	        save_interval=cfg.training.save_interval,
744	        idle_timeout_s=float(os.environ.get("DISAGG_IDLE_TIMEOUT", "0")) or None,
745	        metadata_db_path=_consumer_database_path(cfg),
746	        logger=logger,
747	        log_interval=cfg.training.log_interval,
748	        strategy_kwargs=bundle.strategy_kwargs,
749	        max_checkpoints=cfg.training.max_checkpoints,
750	        tp_size=cfg.training.tp_size,
751	        sp_ulysses_size=cfg.training.sp_ulysses_size,
752	        sp_ring_size=cfg.training.sp_ring_size,
753	        inbox_dir=os.environ.get("DISAGG_INBOX_DIR") or None,
754	        resume_from=cfg.training.resume_from,
755	        dataloader_num_workers=_dataloader_num_workers(cfg, algorithm),
756	        profiling_options=_profiling_options(cfg),
757	    )
758	
759	    return TrainingRun(trainer=trainer)
760	
761	
762	def build_disaggregated_run(
763	    cfg: Config,
764	    *,
765	    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
766	    build_model_bundle: Callable,
767	    prepare_prompts: Callable,
768	    optimizer_factory: Callable,
769	    logger: Callable,
770	):
771	    """Assemble the configured producer or consumer role."""
772	    if cfg.training.role not in ("producer", "consumer"):
773	        raise ValueError(
774	            "disaggregated runs require training.role=producer or consumer"
775	        )
776	    try:
777	        if cfg.mode == "offline":
778	            return _build_offline(
779	                cfg,
780	                algorithm=algorithm,
781	                build_model_bundle=build_model_bundle,
782	                optimizer_factory=optimizer_factory,
783	                logger=logger,
784	            )
785	        return _build_online(
786	            cfg,
787	            algorithm=algorithm,
788	            build_model_bundle=build_model_bundle,
789	            prepare_prompts=prepare_prompts,
790	            optimizer_factory=optimizer_factory,
791	            logger=logger,
792	        )
793	    except BaseException as exc:
794	        _publish_role_assembly_failure(cfg, exc)
795	        raise
796	
797	
798	__all__ = ["build_disaggregated_run"]
799

# coding=utf-8
# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
"""Disaggregated run assembly used by ``specforge train``.

The role is configuration, not a different Python entry point. Producer and
consumer processes load the same run file with ``training.role`` set to their
respective role. Cross-process transport values intentionally remain environment
variables because they are deployment secrets/addresses rather than model
hyperparameters.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
import time
from typing import Callable, Optional, Sequence

from specforge.algorithms.registry import AlgorithmRegistration
from specforge.config import Config

_PRODUCER_CLAIM_SUFFIX = ".producer_claim"
_ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX = ".schedule.json"
_ONLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES = (
    ".closed",
    ".consumed_count",
    ".failed",
    ".consumer_done",
    ".consumer_failed",
    ".consumer_quantum",
    _ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX,
)
_OFFLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES = (".done", ".consumed", ".failed", ".consumer_failed")


def _write_control(path: str, value: str = "") -> None:
    """Atomically publish one small filesystem control record."""
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path)), exist_ok=True)
    tmp = f"{path}.tmp.{os.getpid()}"
    with open(tmp, "w", encoding="utf-8") as stream:
        stream.write(value)
        stream.flush()
        os.fsync(stream.fileno())
    os.replace(tmp, path)


def _read_control(path: str) -> Optional[str]:
    try:
        with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as stream:
            return stream.read().strip() or "unknown remote failure"
    except FileNotFoundError:
        return None


def _publish_control_failure(path: str, exc: BaseException) -> None:
    """Best-effort peer notification that never hides the root exception."""
    try:
        _write_control(path, f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
    except Exception as signal_exc:
        print(
            f"failed to publish role failure to {path}: {signal_exc}",
            flush=True,
        )


def _claim_fresh_control_path(path: str, suffixes: Sequence[str]) -> None:
    """Reject artifact reuse and atomically claim a new producer attempt."""
    claim = path + _PRODUCER_CLAIM_SUFFIX
    artifacts = [path, claim, *(path + suffix for suffix in suffixes)]
    existing = [item for item in artifacts if os.path.exists(item)]
    if existing:
        raise ValueError(
            f"control path {path!r} has artifacts from an existing attempt: "
            f"{existing}; choose a new attempt-specific path"
        )
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path)), exist_ok=True)
    try:
        fd = os.open(claim, os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
    except FileExistsError as exc:
        raise ValueError(
            f"control path {path!r} is already claimed by another producer; "
            "choose a new attempt-specific path"
        ) from exc
    with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as stream:
        stream.write(f"pid={os.getpid()}\n")
        stream.flush()
        os.fsync(stream.fileno())


def _publish_role_assembly_failure(cfg: Config, exc: BaseException) -> None:
    """Notify the peer when a role fails before its run closure is returned.

    Producer notification is allowed only after this process successfully
    claimed the attempt path.  That prevents a typo/retry using an occupied
    path from poisoning another live producer's channel.
    """
    env_name = "DISAGG_MANIFEST" if cfg.mode == "offline" else "DISAGG_REF_CHANNEL"
    path = os.environ.get(env_name)
    if not path:
        return
    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
        claim = _read_control(path + _PRODUCER_CLAIM_SUFFIX)
        if claim != f"pid={os.getpid()}":
            return
        failure_path = path + ".failed"
    else:
        failure_path = path + ".consumer_failed"
    _publish_control_failure(failure_path, exc)


def _primary_rank() -> bool:
    return int(os.environ.get("RANK", "0")) == 0


def _env(name: str) -> str:
    value = os.environ.get(name)
    if not value:
        raise ValueError(f"disaggregated training requires environment variable {name}")
    return value


def _mooncake_store(cfg: Config, *, retain_on_release: bool = False):
    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disaggregated import AuthPolicy
    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.mooncake_store import MooncakeFeatureStore

    token = os.environ.get("DISAGG_AUTH_TOKEN") or None
    setup_kwargs = {
        "local_hostname": os.environ.get("MOONCAKE_LOCAL_HOSTNAME", "127.0.0.1"),
        "metadata_server": _env("MOONCAKE_METADATA_SERVER"),
        "master_server_addr": _env("MOONCAKE_MASTER_SERVER_ADDR"),
        "protocol": os.environ.get("MOONCAKE_PROTOCOL", "tcp"),
        "rdma_devices": os.environ.get("MOONCAKE_RDMA_DEVICES", ""),
    }
    for env_name, key in (
        ("MOONCAKE_GLOBAL_SEGMENT_SIZE", "global_segment_size"),
        ("DISAGG_CLIENT_SEGMENT_SIZE", "global_segment_size"),
        ("MOONCAKE_LOCAL_BUFFER_SIZE", "local_buffer_size"),
        ("DISAGG_CLIENT_BUFFER_SIZE", "local_buffer_size"),
    ):
        if os.environ.get(env_name):
            setup_kwargs[key] = int(os.environ[env_name])
    return MooncakeFeatureStore(
        store_id=os.environ.get("DISAGG_STORE_ID", cfg.run_id),
        setup_kwargs=setup_kwargs,
        auth=AuthPolicy(token),
        credential=token,
        retain_on_release=retain_on_release,
    )


def _offline_store(cfg: Config, *, retain_on_release: bool = False):
    backend = os.environ.get("DISAGG_BACKEND", "shared_dir")
    if backend == "mooncake":
        return _mooncake_store(cfg, retain_on_release=retain_on_release)
    if backend != "shared_dir":
        raise ValueError(
            f"unknown DISAGG_BACKEND={backend!r}; expected shared_dir or mooncake"
        )
    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disaggregated import (
        AuthPolicy,
        SharedDirFeatureStore,
    )

    token = os.environ.get("DISAGG_AUTH_TOKEN") or None
    return SharedDirFeatureStore(
        _env("DISAGG_STORE_ROOT"),
        store_id=os.environ.get("DISAGG_STORE_ID", cfg.run_id),
        auth=AuthPolicy(token),
        credential=token,
        retain_on_release=retain_on_release,
    )


def _server_urls(cfg: Config) -> list[str]:
    deployment = cfg.deployment.disaggregated
    if deployment is not None and deployment.server_urls:
        return list(deployment.server_urls)
    raw = os.environ.get("DISAGG_SERVER_URLS") or os.environ.get("DISAGG_SERVER_URL")
    if not raw:
        raise ValueError(
            "online producer requires deployment.disaggregated.server_urls, "
            "DISAGG_SERVER_URLS, or DISAGG_SERVER_URL"
        )
    return [item.strip() for item in raw.split(",") if item.strip()]


def _consumer_database_path(cfg: Config) -> Optional[str]:
    configured = os.environ.get("DISAGG_DB")
    if configured:
        return configured
    deployment = cfg.deployment.disaggregated
    if deployment is None:
        return None
    state_dir = deployment.consumer_state_dir or deployment.control_dir
    return os.path.join(state_dir, "consumer.sqlite")


def _online_schedule_payload(cfg: Config, *, num_prompts: int) -> dict:
    """Describe the exact finite online schedule prepared by the producer."""
    from specforge.training.schedule import resolve_online_total_steps

    trainer = cfg.deployment.trainer
    dp_size = trainer.nnodes * trainer.nproc_per_node
    total_steps = resolve_online_total_steps(
        num_prompts=num_prompts,
        prompt_epochs=cfg.training.num_epochs,
        dp_size=dp_size,
        batch_size=cfg.training.batch_size,
        accumulation_steps=cfg.training.accumulation_steps,
    )
    return {
        "version": 1,
        "total_steps": total_steps,
        "num_prompts": num_prompts,
        "prompt_epochs": cfg.training.num_epochs,
        "prompt_seed": cfg.training.seed,
        "dp_size": dp_size,
        "batch_size": cfg.training.batch_size,
        "accumulation_steps": cfg.training.accumulation_steps,
    }


def _read_online_total_steps(cfg: Config, channel_path: str) -> int:
    """Read and validate the producer-owned online schedule contract."""
    schedule_path = channel_path + _ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX
    _wait_for(
        schedule_path,
        timeout_s=_optional_timeout_s("DISAGG_PEER_WAIT_TIMEOUT"),
        failure_path=channel_path + ".failed",
    )
    try:
        with open(schedule_path, encoding="utf-8") as stream:
            payload = json.load(stream)
    except (OSError, ValueError) as exc:
        raise ValueError(
            f"invalid online schedule record {schedule_path}: {exc}"
        ) from exc

    trainer = cfg.deployment.trainer
    expected = {
        "version": 1,
        "prompt_epochs": cfg.training.num_epochs,
        "prompt_seed": cfg.training.seed,
        "dp_size": trainer.nnodes * trainer.nproc_per_node,
        "batch_size": cfg.training.batch_size,
        "accumulation_steps": cfg.training.accumulation_steps,
    }
    mismatches = {
        key: (payload.get(key), value)
        for key, value in expected.items()
        if payload.get(key) != value
    }
    if mismatches:
        raise ValueError(
            f"online schedule record {schedule_path} does not match this "
            f"consumer configuration: {mismatches}"
        )
    total_steps = payload.get("total_steps")
    if not isinstance(total_steps, int) or total_steps < 1:
        raise ValueError(
            f"online schedule record {schedule_path} has invalid "
            f"total_steps={total_steps!r}"
        )
    return total_steps


def _optional_timeout_s(name: str) -> Optional[float]:
    """Read an explicitly configured positive timeout.

    Long-running producer/consumer coordination is unbounded by default.  A
    deployment may opt into a terminal timeout through the corresponding
    environment variable, but zero and negative values are almost certainly a
    configuration mistake and match neither the schema nor useful semantics.
    """
    raw = os.environ.get(name)
    if raw is None or not raw.strip():
        return None
    try:
        timeout_s = float(raw)
    except ValueError as exc:
        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a positive number, got {raw!r}") from exc
    if timeout_s <= 0:
        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be positive, got {raw!r}")
    return timeout_s


def _wait_for(
    path: str,
    *,
    timeout_s: Optional[float] = None,
    failure_path: Optional[str] = None,
) -> None:
    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout_s if timeout_s is not None else None
    while not os.path.exists(path):
        failure = _read_control(failure_path) if failure_path else None
        if failure is not None:
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"remote role failed while waiting for {path}: {failure}"
            )
        if deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
            raise TimeoutError(f"timed out waiting for {path}")
        time.sleep(0.25)


def _hold_mooncake_producer(manifest: str) -> None:
    if os.environ.get("DISAGG_BACKEND", "shared_dir") != "mooncake":
        return
    consumed = manifest + ".consumed"
    _wait_for(
        consumed,
        timeout_s=_optional_timeout_s("DISAGG_PRODUCER_HOLD_S"),
        failure_path=manifest + ".consumer_failed",
    )


def _cleanup_offline_mooncake_refs(store, refs: Sequence, *, reason: str) -> None:
    """Remove every object owned by one terminal offline producer attempt."""
    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.feature_store import drain_feature_store_removals

    cleanup_errors = []
    for ref in refs:
        try:
            store.abort(ref.sample_id, reason=reason)
        except Exception as exc:
            cleanup_errors.append(f"{ref.sample_id}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
    try:
        drain_feature_store_removals(store)
    except Exception as exc:
        cleanup_errors.append(f"pending-remove drain: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
    if cleanup_errors:
        raise RuntimeError(
            "offline Mooncake cleanup did not remove every ingested sample: "
            f"{cleanup_errors}"
        )


def _build_offline(
    cfg: Config,
    *,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
    build_model_bundle: Callable,
    optimizer_factory: Callable,
    logger: Callable,
):
    from specforge.training.assembly import (
        TrainingRun,
        _dataloader_num_workers,
        _profiling_options,
    )

    manifest = _env("DISAGG_MANIFEST")
    done = manifest + ".done"

    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
        _claim_fresh_control_path(manifest, _OFFLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES)

        def produce() -> int:
            from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disagg_ingest import (
                ingest_offline_features,
                write_ref_manifest,
            )

            store = None
            tracked_refs = []
            produced = 0
            primary_exc = None
            try:
                store = _offline_store(cfg)
                refs = ingest_offline_features(
                    store,
                    cfg.data.hidden_states_path,
                    algorithm_name=algorithm.name,
                    build_reader=algorithm.providers.offline_for(
                        cfg.model.input_modality
                    ).build_reader,
                    run_id=cfg.run_id,
                    ttt_length=cfg.training.ttt_length,
                    max_len=cfg.data.max_length,
                    on_ref=tracked_refs.append,
                )
                produced = len(refs)
                write_ref_manifest(refs, manifest)
                _write_control(done)
                _hold_mooncake_producer(manifest)
            except BaseException as exc:
                primary_exc = exc
                # Publish the failure sentinel BEFORE the Mooncake abort sweep:
                # the sweep can take minutes over thousands of refs, and a
                # supervisor SIGKILL at grace expiry mid-sweep must not leave a
                # remote consumer waiting forever on a sentinel that never
                # arrives. A cleanup failure below overwrites this record with
                # the combined error.
                _publish_control_failure(manifest + ".failed", primary_exc)

            cleanup_exc = None
            if (
                store is not None
                and os.environ.get("DISAGG_BACKEND", "shared_dir") == "mooncake"
            ):
                try:
                    _cleanup_offline_mooncake_refs(
                        store,
                        tracked_refs,
                        reason=(
                            "offline-attempt-failed"
                            if primary_exc is not None
                            else "offline-attempt-finished"
                        ),
                    )
                except Exception as exc:
                    cleanup_exc = exc

            if primary_exc is not None and cleanup_exc is not None:
                combined = RuntimeError(
                    f"offline producer failed ({type(primary_exc).__name__}: "
                    f"{primary_exc}) and Mooncake cleanup also failed "
                    f"({type(cleanup_exc).__name__}: {cleanup_exc})"
                )
                _publish_control_failure(manifest + ".failed", combined)
                raise combined from primary_exc
            if primary_exc is not None:
                raise primary_exc
            if cleanup_exc is not None:
                _publish_control_failure(manifest + ".failed", cleanup_exc)
                raise cleanup_exc
            return produced

        return TrainingRun(execute=produce)

    _wait_for(done, failure_path=manifest + ".failed")
    from specforge.launch import build_disagg_offline_runtime
    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disagg_ingest import read_ref_manifest

    bundle = build_model_bundle(cfg)
    accumulation_steps = cfg.training.accumulation_steps
    if cfg.training.attention_backend == "usp":
        accumulation_steps *= cfg.training.sp_ulysses_size * cfg.training.sp_ring_size
    trainer = build_disagg_offline_runtime(
        algorithm=algorithm,
        modality=cfg.model.input_modality,
        feature_store=_offline_store(cfg, retain_on_release=True),
        refs=read_ref_manifest(manifest),
        draft_model=bundle.model,
        target_head=bundle.target_head,
        optimizer_factory=optimizer_factory(cfg),
        run_id=cfg.run_id,
        output_dir=cfg.output_dir,
        ttt_length=cfg.training.ttt_length,
        max_len=cfg.data.max_length,
        batch_size=cfg.training.batch_size,
        accumulation_steps=accumulation_steps,
        num_epochs=cfg.training.num_epochs,
        max_steps=cfg.training.max_steps,
        total_steps=cfg.training.total_steps,
        save_interval=cfg.training.save_interval,
        eval_interval=cfg.training.eval_interval,
        eval_hidden_states_path=cfg.data.eval_hidden_states_path or None,
        logger=logger,
        log_interval=cfg.training.log_interval,
        strategy_kwargs=bundle.strategy_kwargs,
        resume_from=cfg.training.resume_from,
        max_checkpoints=cfg.training.max_checkpoints,
        tp_size=cfg.training.tp_size,
        sp_ulysses_size=cfg.training.sp_ulysses_size,
        sp_ring_size=cfg.training.sp_ring_size,
        use_usp_preprocess=(cfg.training.attention_backend == "usp"),
        seed=cfg.training.seed,
        dataloader_num_workers=_dataloader_num_workers(cfg, algorithm),
        profiling_options=_profiling_options(cfg),
    )

    def mark_consumed(_step: int) -> None:
        if _primary_rank():
            _write_control(manifest + ".consumed")

    def mark_consumer_failed(exc: BaseException) -> None:
        _publish_control_failure(manifest + ".consumer_failed", exc)

    return TrainingRun(
        trainer=trainer,
        on_success=mark_consumed,
        on_failure=mark_consumer_failed,
    )


def _producer_capture_metadata(cfg: Config, algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration):
    from specforge.training.capture_contract import resolve_server_capture_contract

    contract = resolve_server_capture_contract(cfg, algorithm=algorithm)
    return (
        list(contract.aux_layer_ids),
        contract.target_hidden_size,
        contract.target_vocab_size,
        contract.draft_vocab_size,
    )


def _build_online(
    cfg: Config,
    *,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
    build_model_bundle: Callable,
    prepare_prompts: Callable,
    optimizer_factory: Callable,
    logger: Callable,
):
    from specforge.training.assembly import (
        TrainingRun,
        _dataloader_num_workers,
        _load_input_tools,
        _profiling_options,
    )

    modality = cfg.model.input_modality
    streaming = algorithm.providers.server_streaming_for(modality)
    channel_path = _env("DISAGG_REF_CHANNEL")
    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
        _claim_fresh_control_path(channel_path, _ONLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES)
    # The producer owns capture and explicit attempt cleanup. The consumer must
    # retain materialized features until DPAckController commits the optimizer
    # boundary and explicitly aborts the acknowledged ids.
    store = _mooncake_store(cfg, retain_on_release=cfg.training.role == "consumer")
    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.feature_store import drain_feature_store_removals
    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.streaming_ref_channel import StreamingRefChannel

    channel = StreamingRefChannel(channel_path)

    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
        from specforge.inference.adapters.server_capture import (
            ServerCaptureSchema,
            SGLangServerCaptureAdapter,
        )
        from specforge.launch import build_disagg_online_producer
        from specforge.training.model_loading import resolve_draft_config

        input_adapter = streaming.create_input_adapter(cfg)
        input_tools = _load_input_tools(
            cfg,
            algorithm,
            input_adapter=input_adapter,
        )
        draft_config = resolve_draft_config(
            cfg,
            provider=algorithm.providers.model.draft_config,
        )
        if input_adapter is None:
            prompts = prepare_prompts(
                cfg,
                input_tools,
                draft_config=draft_config,
            )
        else:
            prompts = input_adapter.prepare_prompts(
                cfg,
                input_tools,
                draft_config=draft_config,
            )
        if cfg.training.total_steps is None and cfg.training.max_steps is None:
            schedule = _online_schedule_payload(cfg, num_prompts=len(prompts))
            _write_control(
                channel_path + _ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX,
                json.dumps(schedule, sort_keys=True),
            )
        layers, hidden_size, target_vocab, draft_vocab = _producer_capture_metadata(
            cfg, algorithm
        )
        layout = streaming.layout
        capture_schema = ServerCaptureSchema(
            aux_feature=layout.aux_feature,
            last_hidden_feature=layout.last_hidden_feature,
            passthrough=layout.passthrough,
            attention_mask_feature=layout.attention_mask_feature,
        )
        adapters = [
            SGLangServerCaptureAdapter(
                url,
                store,
                run_id=cfg.run_id,
                algorithm=algorithm.name,
                schema=capture_schema,
                request_input_adapter=input_adapter,
                target_model_version=cfg.model.target_model_path,
            )
            for url in _server_urls(cfg)
        ]
        target_repr = streaming.target_representation
        peer_wait_timeout_s = _optional_timeout_s("DISAGG_PEER_WAIT_TIMEOUT")
        high_watermark_override = os.environ.get("DISAGG_IN_FLIGHT_HIGH_WATERMARK")
        in_flight_high_watermark = int(
            high_watermark_override or cfg.runtime.in_flight_high_watermark
        )
        low_watermark_override = os.environ.get("DISAGG_IN_FLIGHT_LOW_WATERMARK")
        # Preserve the legacy one-watermark environment override: when only the
        # old high value is supplied, resume at that same threshold.
        in_flight_low_watermark = (
            int(low_watermark_override)
            if low_watermark_override is not None
            else (
                None
                if high_watermark_override is not None
                else cfg.runtime.in_flight_low_watermark
            )
        )
        _workers, drive = build_disagg_online_producer(
            algorithm=algorithm,
            modality=modality,
            prompts=prompts,
            feature_store=store,
            channel=channel,
            feature_source=adapters if len(adapters) > 1 else adapters[0],
            num_rollout_workers=len(adapters),
            run_id=cfg.run_id,
            target_hidden_size=hidden_size,
            target_vocab_size=target_vocab,
            draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab,
            target_repr=target_repr,
            aux_hidden_state_layer_ids=layers,
            prompt_epochs=cfg.training.num_epochs,
            prompt_seed=cfg.training.seed,
            lease=cfg.runtime.producer_lease,
            in_flight_high_watermark=in_flight_high_watermark,
            in_flight_low_watermark=in_flight_low_watermark,
            resident_high_watermark_bytes=(
                int(os.environ["DISAGG_RESIDENT_HIGH_WATERMARK_BYTES"])
                if os.environ.get("DISAGG_RESIDENT_HIGH_WATERMARK_BYTES")
                else cfg.runtime.resident_high_watermark_bytes
            ),
            resident_low_watermark_bytes=(
                int(os.environ["DISAGG_RESIDENT_LOW_WATERMARK_BYTES"])
                if os.environ.get("DISAGG_RESIDENT_LOW_WATERMARK_BYTES")
                else cfg.runtime.resident_low_watermark_bytes
            ),
            feature_store_max_resident_bytes=(
                cfg.runtime.feature_store_max_resident_bytes
            ),
            peer_wait_timeout_s=peer_wait_timeout_s,
        )

        def produce() -> int:
            produced = 0
            primary_exc = None
            try:
                produced = drive(should_stop=channel.consumer_stopped)
                deadline = (
                    time.monotonic() + peer_wait_timeout_s
                    if peer_wait_timeout_s is not None
                    else None
                )
                while not channel.consumer_stopped():
                    if deadline is not None and time.monotonic() >= deadline:
                        raise TimeoutError(
                            "producer timed out waiting for the consumer result "
                            f"after {peer_wait_timeout_s:.0f}s"
                        )
                    time.sleep(0.25)
                consumer_failure = channel.consumer_failure()
                if consumer_failure is not None:
                    raise RuntimeError(f"consumer failed: {consumer_failure}")
            except BaseException as exc:
                primary_exc = exc
                try:
                    channel.fail(f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
                except Exception as signal_exc:
                    print(
                        f"failed to publish producer failure: {signal_exc}",
                        flush=True,
                    )
            cleanup_errors = []
            try:
                reader = StreamingRefChannel(channel_path)
                while True:
                    refs = reader.poll(max_n=1024)
                    if not refs:
                        break
                    for ref in refs:
                        try:
                            store.abort(ref.sample_id, reason="online-attempt-finished")
                        except Exception as exc:
                            cleanup_errors.append(
                                f"{ref.sample_id}: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
                            )
            except Exception as exc:
                cleanup_errors.append(
                    f"published-ref scan: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
                )
            try:
                store.discard_external_attempts(
                    reason="online-attempt-unadopted-capture"
                )
            except Exception as exc:
                cleanup_errors.append(
                    f"unadopted-capture cleanup: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
                )
            try:
                drain_feature_store_removals(store)
            except Exception as exc:
                cleanup_errors.append(
                    f"pending-remove drain: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
                )
            if primary_exc is not None and cleanup_errors:
                raise RuntimeError(
                    f"producer failed ({type(primary_exc).__name__}: "
                    f"{primary_exc}) and Mooncake cleanup also failed: "
                    f"{cleanup_errors}"
                ) from primary_exc
            if primary_exc is not None:
                raise primary_exc
            if cleanup_errors:
                raise RuntimeError(
                    "producer could not clean all published Mooncake features: "
                    f"{cleanup_errors}"
                )
            return produced

        return TrainingRun(execute=produce)

    from specforge.launch import build_disagg_online_consumer

    total_steps = cfg.training.total_steps
    if total_steps is None and cfg.training.max_steps is None:
        total_steps = _read_online_total_steps(cfg, channel_path)
    bundle = build_model_bundle(cfg)
    trainer = build_disagg_online_consumer(
        algorithm=algorithm,
        modality=modality,
        feature_store=store,
        channel=channel,
        draft_model=bundle.model,
        target_head=bundle.target_head,
        optimizer_factory=optimizer_factory(cfg),
        run_id=cfg.run_id,
        output_dir=cfg.output_dir,
        batch_size=cfg.training.batch_size,
        accumulation_steps=cfg.training.accumulation_steps,
        max_steps=cfg.training.max_steps,
        total_steps=total_steps,
        save_interval=cfg.training.save_interval,
        idle_timeout_s=float(os.environ.get("DISAGG_IDLE_TIMEOUT", "0")) or None,
        metadata_db_path=_consumer_database_path(cfg),
        logger=logger,
        log_interval=cfg.training.log_interval,
        strategy_kwargs=bundle.strategy_kwargs,
        max_checkpoints=cfg.training.max_checkpoints,
        tp_size=cfg.training.tp_size,
        sp_ulysses_size=cfg.training.sp_ulysses_size,
        sp_ring_size=cfg.training.sp_ring_size,
        inbox_dir=os.environ.get("DISAGG_INBOX_DIR") or None,
        resume_from=cfg.training.resume_from,
        dataloader_num_workers=_dataloader_num_workers(cfg, algorithm),
        profiling_options=_profiling_options(cfg),
    )

    return TrainingRun(trainer=trainer)


def build_disaggregated_run(
    cfg: Config,
    *,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
    build_model_bundle: Callable,
    prepare_prompts: Callable,
    optimizer_factory: Callable,
    logger: Callable,
):
    """Assemble the configured producer or consumer role."""
    if cfg.training.role not in ("producer", "consumer"):
        raise ValueError(
            "disaggregated runs require training.role=producer or consumer"
        )
    try:
        if cfg.mode == "offline":
            return _build_offline(
                cfg,
                algorithm=algorithm,
                build_model_bundle=build_model_bundle,
                optimizer_factory=optimizer_factory,
                logger=logger,
            )
        return _build_online(
            cfg,
            algorithm=algorithm,
            build_model_bundle=build_model_bundle,
            prepare_prompts=prepare_prompts,
            optimizer_factory=optimizer_factory,
            logger=logger,
        )
    except BaseException as exc:
        _publish_role_assembly_failure(cfg, exc)
        raise


__all__ = ["build_disaggregated_run"]

The controller and feature store are lock-protected; the channel is
        not, so publishes serialize through ``publish_lock``.
        """
        from collections import deque

        drive_start = time.perf_counter()
        progress_interval = float(
            os.environ.get("DISAGG_PRODUCER_PROGRESS_INTERVAL", 30.0)
        )
        producer_timing(
            "drive_producer enter "
            f"workers={len(workers)} lease={worker_lease} max_rounds={max_rounds} "
            f"watermarks={in_flight_high_watermark}/"
            f"{flow_control.limits.resolved_low_watermark_refs} "
            f"progress_interval={progress_interval}"
        )
        quantum_wait_start = time.monotonic()
        try:
            while True:
                consumer_quantum = channel.consumer_quantum()
                if consumer_quantum is not None:
                    break
                consumer_failure = channel.consumer_failure()
                if consumer_failure is not None:
                    raise RuntimeError(
                        "consumer failed before publishing its optimizer window: "
                        f"{consumer_failure}"
                    )
                if (
                    peer_wait_timeout_s is not None
                    and time.monotonic() - quantum_wait_start > peer_wait_timeout_s
                ):
                    raise TimeoutError(
                        "producer timed out waiting for the consumer optimizer "
                        f"window after {peer_wait_timeout_s:.0f}s"
                    )
                sleep(backpressure_poll_s)
            if in_flight_high_watermark < consumer_quantum:
                raise ValueError(
                    "producer in-flight high watermark "
                    f"{in_flight_high_watermark} is smaller than the consumer's "
                    f"global optimizer-step quantum {consumer_quantum}; set "
                    "DISAGG_IN_FLIGHT_HIGH_WATERMARK to at least that value"
                )
            resolved_low_watermark = flow_control.limits.resolved_low_watermark_refs
            if resolved_low_watermark < consumer_quantum:
                # The consumer dispatches only whole optimizer windows, so a
                # paused producer must be resumable while the consumer still
                # needs up to one full window: a low watermark below the
                # quantum could leave both sides waiting on each other.
                raise ValueError(
                    "producer in-flight low watermark "
                    f"{resolved_low_watermark} is smaller than the consumer's "
                    f"global optimizer-step quantum {consumer_quantum}; set "
                    "DISAGG_IN_FLIGHT_LOW_WATERMARK to at least that value"
                )
            producer_timing(
                f"consumer optimizer window ready quantum={consumer_quantum}"
            )
        except BaseException as exc:
            try:
                channel.fail(f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
            except Exception:
                logger.exception("failed to publish producer setup failure")
            raise
        for w in workers:
            producer_timing(f"rollout worker start worker_id={w.worker_id}")
            w.start()
        publish_lock = threading.Lock()
        state = {
            "produced": 0,
            "first_ref_logged": False,
            "accounted_consumed": channel.consumed_remote(),
            "resident_bytes": 0,
        }
        published_sizes = deque()
        last_publish_log = {"t": time.perf_counter()}
        dead: dict = {}  # worker_id -> last failure reason

        def pool_drained() -> bool:
            st = controller.status()
            # leased counts too: a peer's in-flight lease may fail retryable
            # and come back — leaving then would strand it.
            return st["prompts_pending"] == 0 and st["prompts_leased"] == 0

        def reconcile_consumed_locked() -> int:
            consumed = channel.consumed_remote()
            delta = consumed - state["accounted_consumed"]
            if delta < 0 or delta > len(published_sizes):
                raise RuntimeError(
                    "producer byte accounting does not match the channel: "
                    f"consumed advanced by {delta} with "
                    f"{len(published_sizes)} published refs tracked"
                )
            for _ in range(delta):
                state["resident_bytes"] -= published_sizes.popleft()
            state["accounted_consumed"] = consumed
            return state["resident_bytes"]

        def resident_bytes() -> int:
            # Mooncake health is process-local and cannot observe deletes made by
            # a remote consumer. Track the published-but-unacknowledged prefix
            # from SampleRef.estimated_bytes and the channel's durable counter.
            with publish_lock:
                return reconcile_consumed_locked()

        def run_worker(w) -> None:
            failures = 0
            last_backpressure_log = 0.0
            for _ in range(max_rounds):
                if should_stop is not None and should_stop():
                    return
                _infl = channel.in_flight_remote()
                _resident = resident_bytes()
                # backpressure: in_flight = published - consumer-acked
                backpressure_started = time.monotonic()
                while flow_control.should_pause(
                    in_flight_refs=_infl,
                    resident_bytes=_resident,
                ):
                    now = time.perf_counter()
                    if (
                        progress_interval > 0
                        and now - last_backpressure_log >= progress_interval
                    ):
                        st = controller.status()
                        producer_timing(
                            "backpressure wait "
                            f"worker={w.worker_id} produced={state['produced']} "
                            f"in_flight={channel.in_flight_remote()} "
                            f"resident_bytes={resident_bytes()} "
                            f"pending={st['prompts_pending']} "
                            f"leased={st['prompts_leased']} "
                            f"elapsed={elapsed(drive_start)}"
                        )
                        last_backpressure_log = now
                    if should_stop is not None and should_stop():
                        return
                    if (
                        peer_wait_timeout_s is not None
                        and time.monotonic() - backpressure_started
                        > peer_wait_timeout_s
                    ):
                        raise TimeoutError(
                            "producer backpressure timed out after "
                            f"{peer_wait_timeout_s:.0f}s waiting for consumer "
                            f"progress (in_flight={_infl})"
                        )
                    sleep(backpressure_poll_s)
                    _infl = channel.in_flight_remote()
                    _resident = resident_bytes()
                try:
                    run_once_start = time.perf_counter()
                    refs = w.run_once(max_tasks=worker_lease)
                except Exception as exc:
                    # the worker already failed its leases retryable; peers
                    # (or this worker, next round) will re-lease them.
                    failures += 1
                    logger.warning(
                        "rollout worker %s failed (%d/%d): %s",
                        w.worker_id,
                        failures,
                        max_worker_failures,
                        exc,
                    )
                    if failures >= max_worker_failures:
                        dead[w.worker_id] = str(exc)
                        logger.error(
                            "dropping rollout worker %s after %d consecutive "
                            "failures; health=%s",

1311	def build_disagg_online_consumer(
1312	    *,
1313	    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
1314	    modality: str = "text",
1315	    feature_store: FeatureStore,
1316	    channel,
1317	    draft_model,
1318	    optimizer_factory,
1319	    run_id: str,
1320	    output_dir: str,
1321	    target_head=None,
1322	    batch_size: int = 1,
1323	    accumulation_steps: int = 1,
1324	    max_steps: Optional[int] = None,
1325	    total_steps: Optional[int] = None,
1326	    save_interval: int = 0,
1327	    eval_interval: int = 0,
1328	    eval_data_factory=None,
1329	    collate_fn=None,
1330	    idle_timeout_s: Optional[float] = None,
1331	    metadata_store: Optional[MetadataStore] = None,
1332	    metadata_db_path: Optional[str] = None,
1333	    logger=None,
1334	    log_interval: int = 50,
1335	    strategy_kwargs: Optional[Mapping[str, Any]] = None,
1336	    max_checkpoints: int = 0,
1337	    tp_size: int = 1,
1338	    sp_ulysses_size: int = 1,
1339	    sp_ring_size: int = 1,
1340	    dp_rank: Optional[int] = None,
1341	    dp_size: Optional[int] = None,
1342	    inbox_dir: Optional[str] = None,
1343	    resume_from: Optional[str] = None,
1344	    dataloader_num_workers: int = 0,
1345	    profiling_options=None,
1346	):
1347	    """Consumer (trainer) side of an ONLINE disaggregated run.
1348	
1349	    Rank 0 always runs the :class:`RefDistributor`, including for ``dp_size=1``.
1350	    It is the only reader of ``channel`` and the only writer to the
1351	    ``metadata_store``/``metadata_db_path`` ledger, then dispatches refs into one
1352	    inbox per rank. Every rank consumes the same inbox-based path and durable
1353	    acknowledgements gather to rank 0 through :class:`DPAckController`.
1354	
1355	    ``resume_from`` is consumer-only. Rank 0 reconciles the retained SQLite
1356	    ledger, skips optimizer-durable refs, requeues the unacked tail, and requires
1357	    the durable marker to match the checkpoint step. The producer and original
1358	    data plane must still be available; this does not restart a producer.
1359	    """
1360	    import torch.distributed as dist
1361	
1362	    from specforge.runtime.control_plane.dp_ack import DPAckController
1363	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.ref_distributor import (
1364	        InboxChannel,
1365	        RefDistributor,
1366	    )
1367	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.streaming_ref_channel import StreamingRefQueue
1368
